rows.text: Yayerrrrr.

I've spent the last few months searching for an RPG which combines clever writing, exciting gameplay, meaningful character RPG changes, cared for side quests, a story which takes itself seriously (but not too), and does not actively make its systems opaque. This quest of mine has led me back in time to play Baldur's Gate, Planescape Torment, then a bit further ahead to play Fallout New Vegas, Yakuza 0, Pillars of Eternity 1 and 2, then even to modern times to play BG3, Wasteland 3. I had already played Divinity 2, Skyrim, Fallout 4, and Cyberpunk 2077. Then, I thought "well, I'll play witcher 2, since I already played Witcher 3." And I can say with confidence that of all those games, Witcher 3 is still my favorite. It does just enough of everything. Is it perfect? No, but it's more fun than almost any other RPG available right now. If I were to pick one between 2077 and this, I'd pick this if I was on a budget (it's so cheap). Personally, I like 2077's aesthetic more (I'm a cyberpunk nerd) but Witcher 3 is just...perfect for what it is. And, more importantly, what I found on my quest is that, just because the game isn't perfect doesn't mean there's another game which is. Every game has faults, systems and details that aren't implemented well, graphical problems, and the like. What you want is a game which grabs you despite those faults. That's what W3 is.

PIrated the game 2 years ago to see what the fuss is about. Finished it. Went back, bought and played Witcher 1 and 2. Then bought this and now am playing the whole thing all over again. There are very few games that come close to Halo in defining the genre they belong to. The Witcher games are among them. I would strongly recommend that you play Witcher 1 and 2 before playing Witcher 3. While the gameplay mechanics in the earlier games are a bit clumsy, the story more than makes up for it.
10/10 on all aspects except maybe the combat grind.

Pros:
 - A rich and complete RPG experience with a wide variety of high quality quests.
- An ambitious scope that was very expertly executed, and nearly incomparable to anything else made so far. 
- The quest quality is pretty consistent throughout the game, even over a hundred hours in, you'd think the game wouldn't be able to keep up with the level of detail that it offers in the first hours, yet it does exactly that and then some.
- Great story and writing, from the main quest to the smaller quests and the stories and characters that you'll encounter, lots of detail and love were poured all over this world, some of which you might not even encounter on your first playthrough. Any and all sorts of emotions and moments that you'll probably remember for years to come.
- Decisions you make have a pretty substantial impact in the story and on other characters, making you feel the weight of responsibility, some of the choices are surprisingly difficult to make.
- Substantially different endings based on your choices.
- The game doesn't pull many punches when it comes to the number romantic options that it offers, some will even influence certain aspects of the story, it's romantic when it needs to be, it's sexy when it needs to be, and at times pretty funny.
- Gameplay has taken what was made in the earlier installments, build upon it and create a very polished and intuitive improvement, the combat feels amazing and personal, with you being able to find your own play style and groove. It plays like a smooth action-adventure game despite being a massive and comprehensive RPG.
- A character upgrade system that will allow you to improve Geralt's fighting, signs (which are basically spells) and alchemy which influences your effectiveness with potions and bombs. Offering more in the way of play-style customization.
- Tons of different weapons and gear that you can use, as-well to different effects that you can add.
- The visuals are great, with an amazing art direction, I have no doubt that this game will age pretty well.
- The world is beautiful to look at, from the different cities, to small villages, islands, mountains and forests, this game has some of the most realistic forests and green environments that I've seen in game, there's a really nice chaotic feel of how trees and plants are placed which makes it feel very natural. The weather system directly impacts how everything looks and the world and even NPCs react to it accordingly. One of the best day/night cycles that I've seen in a open world, dusk and sun rise can at times look absolutely stunning.
- Beautiful music throughout the game.
- Great DLCs that add tens of hours of content, none of it feels lazy or by the numbers, instead it feels like genuine contributions to the world, with some of the writing and quests being of higher quality than even most of what we've encountered in the main game, which was already pretty good. Blood and Wine in particular is probably the single best story-based DLC that I've seen in a game. 
- Gwent is an awesome addition to the franchise, and far superior to the prior mini-games.

Cons:

- I've experience multiple crashes, and by multiple, I really mean it. This game crashed a lot. And I know for a fact that this is a game issue and not a system issue, because this level of frequent crashing only happened when played The Witcher 3, in fact, I have yet to experience this problem on their newest Cyberpunk game or on any other game that I'm currently playing. Very unfortunate and by my far my biggest issue with this game and what prevents it from being a 10/10 in my eyes.

- The in-game economy is very unbalanced, I find myself very often finding most of the weapons and gear that I need just by exploring the world, I consistently found swords that were an immediate upgrade to swords that I've just picked not long ago, giving me very little incentive to explore crafting or buy weapons from stores, if anything, I mostly used stores to get gold from selling the excess weapons and gear that I would frequently find in the game world. The exception being when I wanted a particular armor that I would only get through crafting.

- The menus and in-game interface although you'll eventually get used to it, its not the most intuitive.

- The game could have offered more way for me to spend my gold, like buying houses, decoration and properties. The Blood and Wine DLC introduces some of that in the game, thought because you're supposed to play it after the main campaign, it all comes a bit too late. Would be awesome to have had a place to improve and decorate with different items from my different adventures, earlier in the game, maybe even have some of the main characters drop by. Though I do realize this is less of a flaw and more of a personal wish and nitpick.

Overall:

The Witcher 3 is arguably one of the best RPGs ever made, if not the best, if you're a fan of the genre or if you are curious about what a CDPR game looks at its best, I definitely recommend you to skip on Cyberpunk 2077 until they fix it, and instead dive into the world of the Witcher and learn exactly why this studio became so beloved by so many gamers. This is an awesome journey that has pretty much everything a game could offer, an immersive world and story that will keep you interested, lots to do and explore and you'll always feel in control of the pace of which you want to experience the game. If you want to pick up the game to just do some quick Witcher contracts and slash some monsters, you can, if you want to just walk, swim, ride and sail around the massive world and probably always finding something in the process, you can. If you want to just play Gwent and collect new cards from NPCs you can, and if you want to experience one of the best video-game stories and story-based quests ever written and designed, The Witcher 3 will be there for you with very little risk of leaving you disappointed.

Настало время написать не менее легендарный обзорчик о легендарной игре.
Ну начнем с того что в далеком 2007 года CD Projekt Red выпускает в свет 1 Ведьмака,при создании первой части производитель видимо ориентировался на что-то приближенное Gotich 3 НО не допустил главное ошибки какую допускают почти все производители нашего времени,копирка.
1 Часть зашла далеко не всем,нельзя было переодевать персонажа,боевка была не понраву многим,НО всё-таки она запала многим в душу.Прошло достаточно много времени с момента выхода первой части и тут в 2012 году выходит Ведьмак 2 Убийца королей,Вот тут то все и начали фанатеть по вселеной Ведьмака,шикарный сюжет,продолжение истории Мясника из Блавикена который до сих пор пытается востановить память паралельно спасая всех на своем пути и зарабатывая пару десятков оренов.Прошло 3 года после выхода 2 части и в 2015 году мы получаем легендарную Ведьмак 3 Дикая охота:Система переноса данных из прошлых частей,сюжет,да даже просто контракты в деревушках на окрайне,Легендарно.Дополнения:Кровь и вино,Каменные сердца.Шедевр.Право выбора с кем быть и кого убить,у кого взять задание а кого послать подальше,всё на высшем уровне.Вот и закончилась история Ведьмака Геральта из Ривии,предназначеное ему дитя по праву неожиданности вернулось а Йен наконец-то нашлась.

Words can't describe how amazing this game is. Developers really just don't put the time in effort into games like this anymore. The lore is truly amazing.

For those just starting the game, even though the main story doesn't pick up right away, the side missions are wonderfully crafted and the characters have depth and your choices matter.

I love being immersed in a game's mechanics in order to defeat enemies more efficiently and the Witcher 3 does this beautifully (bestiary, oils, deconcotions, etc). 

I could say so much more about how amazing this game is, but TLDR:

If you are looking for a game you can sink 100+ hours into easily where you can be rewarded and gratified in the end, this is the game for you.

久仰巫师3大名
一个和老滚5一起被并列为RPG游戏界的两座大山级的作品
老滚5我玩了很长时间，初中时候玩盗版的时间和入正补票的游戏时间加起来得有500多个小时了（这还不算各种mod花的时间）
说实话，我很喜欢那种能给人很强代入感的游戏，仿佛自己就是抓根宝，就是利维亚的杰洛特
我用自己的原则，来衡量在游戏里遇见的人和事
去爱，去恨，去怜悯宽恕一个人，去忿忿不平一件事
显然，多分支走向的剧情，细节饱满，世界观塑造完善的巫师3非常合我的胃口
时而做一个严谨，专业且耐心的猎魔人在山林间追踪妖灵的踪迹
时而是个老好人，脑子一热就去为村里的悲惨老妇人免费跑了腿
又有时做一个多情的女术士打桩机，浪漫，幽默，风流倜傥
然而当一切尘埃散尽，我仍然是我，背着两把剑，在夕阳下的乡间小道上骑着萝卜疾驰而去
闲看庭前花开花落，漫随天外云卷云舒
在战火纷飞的乱世，保有一份独特的内心清净
坚持自己的道义，仗剑走天涯，快哉快哉！

Quite easily the best RPG of all time, even after 5 years from its release. It has everything at a high standard.

1. Looks, from characters to environments everything is beautiful whether it needs to be creepy or mystic
2. Music. lelele lelelele
3. Story, it can speak for itself
4. Characters, they too speak for themselves
5. Combat, while not ground breaking, it compliments the world perfectly as you have to intently focus on whatever you're fighting and watch it trying to murder you.

The only thing that should stop you from playing this is the long time investment to appreciate the world and finish the game(50 hours +), your PC cant run it, or you are 12 (plenty of adult themes)

I just finished the main quest at 80 hrs, and i could have taken more time with all the side quests and witcher contracts, but the writing was amazing, and the graphics were next level at parts... the customization is minimal from some other fantasy rpgs that ive played in the past, and the gameplay was easy to grow accustom to, while not super challenging on the normal difficulty, there were many moments where i was changing up my battle style. Most importantly, save often as the story lines have so many twists and turns that if you skip through a scene too quickly you might quickly regret it. Almost all 80 hours were enjoyable and very little grinding(mostly all by my own accord) 5/5 I have yet to play the dlcs but am looking forward to it, all i can say is take your time with the main quest and have fun the whole way.

I find The Witcher 3 to be a difficult game to judge.

On one hand it's an incredibly overrated game. The world of The Witcher 3 is shallow, it's all appearances which makes it end up feeling hollow with nothing really dynamic going on at all. The combat system feels floaty and janky. The RPG mechanics are shallow and the way the game handles equipment is horrendous. The game is also abandoned by the developers despite there being game breaking bugs left unfixed years later, so beware!

On the other hand the game does have excellent writing and acting with engaging side content. Despite the game marketing itself as an Action RPG I would recommend engaging the game as a visual novel instead. Put the game at whatever difficulty suits you best and immerse yourself in the story since it's the only thing this game does excellently. But since the game fails in many areas that I personally find crucial for an Action RPG I can not recommend this game.

Oh yeah, I also don't get why this was nominated as a Labour of Love. It's been years since the last DLC and they haven't released an official patch in a long time despite there still existing gamebreaking bugs years after release. I get that they're working on a next-gen remaster but god knows when it's going to be released or what state it will be released in.
Great game, highly recommend it.

Wild Hunt invests much more in the slow connection with the player, than in the fast, shallow and "childish" excitement. And I think that was what the series needed. A game that will provide you with rich content, the right RPG mechanics and an interactive, lively and compelling world. The Poles, although they did not need it, rebuilt their relationship with the public, piece by piece, from the beginning. The title starts warmly, highlighting the absolute basics and in the process unfolds its beautiful virtues. If you think about it, it takes guts to radically change the whole philosophy and architecture of a series that was loved. It takes courage to give such a game, which is based on everything in the long run, to an audience that is waiting for its "jaws to go" when it puts the disc in the console. This means that there in Warsaw, some do not rest and certainly do not swim in bathtubs with rose petals. They have a passion, they have an appetite for work, they have a goal and we will do very well to give them the recognition and praise they deserve.

Just finished Cyberpunk 2077...This prompted me to finally review W3 WH.  It's rather difficult to believe the same studio made both.  This game represents the high water mark of game, dialogue and quest writing,  It also does an excelent job with the OW map.  Most OW map type RPGs devolve into a chase the map icon to do list which becomes grindy and tedious instead of engaging and immersive.

A good RPG side tracks you. It draws you in painlessly. It does not waste your time with filler just to clear an  icon.  You fire up the game entirely focused on a specific set of goals; then get sidetracked by a facinating story arc, quest, or activity having nothing to do with what you were aiming for.  Then your wife( or insert SO here) hands you a pillow and a small throw blanket after turning the heat down.  And you have no regrets...well you didn't need all your toes anyway.....


I didn't have this problem with Cyberpunk...The only times I failed to reach my goals for the play session was after my eyes started bleeding on yet another chase the stupid map icon that you otherwise don't give a damn about.  I can't even remember any characters names except mine which is a single letter...sigh  
I haven't played W3 in over a year and I still remember quite a number of quests and side arcs.  I shut off Cyberpunk (for the last time most likely) less than 2 days ago and I can barely remember the high points of the main plot.

When the Witcher 1 came out, I thought the gameplay was.. Acceptable, whereas the story was somewhat confusing and finally I found the setting to be particularly good. 
The Witcher 2 suffered from some of the same issues that the Witcher 1 had - the game was a drag in many ways, but significantly better gameplay wise, though I'm not going to lie - I hated the locations the game took place in. 

The witcher 3 made all the suffering from the previous games worth it. It remains one of my favorite games. 
However, that said, I will not play this game without mods. 
I am not a fan of the game's tendency to reward you with "leveled" items, but fortunately there are mods that fix that.

I have played and beaten this game on xbox 3 times, playstation 1 time, and on PC 2 times. YES this game is worth it. When Witcher 3 first came out I slowly lost interest during the Bloody Baron quest line. I then gave up on the game and played other things. 6-8 months later I decided to start it back up and give it a shot. I do not regret that whatsoever. The game is truly a remarkable experience with AMAZING music, characters, and environment. I loved the game so much I bought the books and read through them all. I played the first Witcher which was VERY different but still worth the buy and time spent in it. I still need to play the second which I will very soon. All in all this game has truly been a remarkable RPG for me and will continue to stay on my PC and I will continue to play this masterpiece every time I want play an amazing game.

Прошло уже почти 6 лет с выхода третьего ведьмака, но до сих пор творение CDP.RED ставят в пример и упоминают в различных топах и обзорах. Ведьмак 3 очень высоко задрал планку качественных ролевых игр и многие считают, что пока никому так и не удалось до неё дотянуться. Я только сейчас взялся за 3ю часть, хотя до этого прошёл и первую и вторую.  Всё что было сказано в сторону этой игры - правда. Тут прекрасный открытый мир с кучей активностей, интересные побочные квесты которые прописаны не хуже сюжетных, остроумные и вариативные диалоги, проработанные персонажи знакомые нам по прошлым частям или книгам, огромное количество лорных существ, событий и спрятанных секретных мест. Отдельно хочу отметить ведьмачьи заказы, их определенно полезно, а главное интересно выполнять. 
Про игру уже сказали за эти годы всё, что только можно. Минусов я лично для себя не нашел. Даже если вы не фанат фентези или Сапковского, не стоит проходить мимо такого интересного, проработанного и захватывающего приключения.

I think after 30 something hours of playing the game I am ready to write down a review. I will try to keep it as concise as possible. 
Story: 
You follow the path of the Witcher and his many adventures. Yes, there is the main quest, but there is also tons of side quests, I am almost finished with the second book "Sword of Destiny" and I feel that the game is capturing the soul and the feel of the books very well. It does feel like playing the Witcher with all the monster contracts, the wretches you meet along the way and the odd turns that you face. It never ceases to be interesting, the world is huge and from what I have seen so far, the story is faithful to the overall lore of the Witcher universe. 
Even if you didn't read the books the story will absorb you nevertheless. 

Graphics:
6 years later and it still looks very beautiful, thankfully, this game depends not only on technical beauty but also artistic beauty which is timeless. It runs very smooth on my GTX 1080Ti @4k with almost everything maxed except for hairworks. 

Sound:
The voice acting is superbly done, probably the best I have ever seen in any game. [spoiler]I was mesmerized specially at the part where the old woman is possessed by the three sisters and they speak through her. [/spoiler]. 
Musical score is also some of the best I heard, it switches between ambient exploration music and in fights it becomes intense folklore music, the vocal parts are amazingly done as well. It also changes to fit the story and the mood. 

Gameplay:
No game is perfect, and I feel that The Witcher 3 falls a bit short on gameplay. The fights, while fun, they are simple, or rather simpler than Witcher 2 and Witcher 1. That is not my issue though, my real issue is with alchemy, it had been dumbed down so much to the point that it became generic. To create a potion, all you have to do is collect the ingredients only once and then your potions and bombs will regenerate every time you meditate through consuming Alcohest which is very simple. 
This greatly kills the immersion for me. Part of the fun in Witcher 1 and 2 was when I meditate before a major hunt and I prepare and drink my potions during the meditation phase. It felt real and it felt like what would a Witcher do before a fight. I know this was an attempt from CDPR to make the game more mainstream but I can live with that. 
As for Gwent, I haven't enjoyed a minigame like this since triple triad on Final Fantasy 8 and 9. It is extremely well done and very very fun and interesting to play. I won't spoil it for you but if you enjoyed triple triad, you will love Gwent. 

Closing words:
It is hard to believe that this game came from the same company that created CB2077, the attention to detail is insane and all the quests feel like they have been handcrafted without exceptions. 
For maximum enjoyment, I highly recommend that you at least play the first two witcher games, you can get them both for pennies.

사고나서 했다가 접었다가 4트째 하고있는데, 스토리고 뭐고 여전히 개 ㅈ같은 조작감때메 스트레스 존나받아서 못해먹겠음

레데리는 묵직함이 있고(조작감이 구리다고 표현하기는 힘듦), 다크소울이나 세키로는 즉각반응이 있는데 이건 이도저도 아닌데 줫나 굼뜨고 답답함. 군대 폐급 후임 보는거보다 더 답답해서 미치겠음 그냥

Great story, interesting characters, engaging card game side quest, but true to its reputation a very long game. Even Geralt sounded tired at times.

The Good: I have been on and off this game several times over the years. Each time I return, I know there is plenty of unexplored world and content awaiting me. Money worth? Definitely. With updated quest journal, beast glossary (and my previously saved mouse key bindings), it really wasn't difficult to get back into the rhythm.

The Bad: Developers obviously do not understand the meaning of the word "Climax" and "Final". All the while I was progressing in the main quest and about to reach the "Final" battle to kick the last boss back to before he was born. And after that close encounter, I find myself still have to go through even more quests to find him to fight him again! Like, I need to go find a dude to ask about some stone, who in turn refer me to two other dudes. Seriously man, if the game does not reach a peak, its called a plateau. Ever wondered why so many players gave up and dropped out of this beloved game? And I think I have been on and about this "Final" main quest for days now! Only this time when I am done typing this review, I hope it will finally be final in maybe another 10, 20 hours? Geez, anticlimax. You don't build up players expectations to drag on.

After I am done with this "final", I need to reconsider starting the purchased expansions immediately after. Need a change in scenery and environment. Like what I said, another on and off the game, again.

I recommend this game because it's a major milestone in open worlds, but I actually don't like it as much as most people do. I'll just hit 4 points:
-Many of your choices from The Witcher 2 don't carry over, especially if you picked Iorveth's path. Iorveth and Saskia are nowhere to be found. I think the devs built this game for Roche's path, and then ran out of time before adding Iorveth's.
-Even though the world is huge, most if it is either empty space, copy-and-pasted monster nests, or locked buildings that are empty and just meant to create the facade of a large civilization.
-Crafting is pointless except for the absolute best weapons/armor. That's because equipment scales by level. So if you spend hours collecting materials to craft a great sword at level 20, after one quest you'll hit level 21 and the sword will be useless.
-Even though you can vary your playstyle, only one playstyle will work well in NG+. That's because some playstyles--like signs--don't scale nearly as well as others from level to level. So a lot of your personal playstyle preferences get tossed aside.

이 게임이 나오자 마자 접한 분들이라면 멋진 그래픽에 까무러쳤을지도 모르지만 지금은 "우와!" 할 정도는 아닐겁니다. 
저는 아직 스켈리게 밖에 못 왔지만 스토리를 만들어 간다고 생각하고 즉흥적으로 선택하여 1회차를 즐기고 있습니다.

어쎄신크리드 오디세이를 하고 접하신 분들은 부르면 달려오는 '포보스'와 달리 어슬렁어슬렁 오는 '로취'와 조금만 높아도 미끄러져 올라가지 못하는 절벽 등 여러가지로 성질이 좀 나겠지만 요즘 게임으로 이미 눈이 높아진 다음에 접한걸 어떡하겠어요.

이런저런 이유로 이 거대한 서사에 빠지기 어렵다면 미리 스토리를 정리한 영상을 보고 시작하면 좋습니다.

'백색과수원' 지역은 튜토리얼 지역이니 위쳐센스 잘 누르시고 퀘스트가 시키는 데로 좀 헤매시면 곧 다음 지역인 '벨렌'으로 올 수 있을 겁니다. 궨트 어려우면 관련 퀘 안해도 아무 문제 없습니다. 

렙업하면 주는 스킬 포인트는 음식을 20분동안 지속적으로 먹게 해주는 것을 최우선으로 올리고 엑시3개 올린뒤 속공에 올인하면 될 것 같습니다.

[h1] TLDR: [/h1] It's okay. If you are looking for an immersive (action) RPG, this is not it. This is not a role-playing game per-se. It has some role playing elements but not in the classical sense of "choose your adventure".  If you are looking for an epic storytelling adventure as a Witcher with wonderful visuals and sound - this is definitely it. Read more for details.

[h1] Long version: [/h1]

[b]My background (for context): [/b]
I am into all kind of RPG and strategy games, been a gamer for over 25 years. I love story-driven RPGs, be those turn-based, isometric, over the shoulder, or action. Doesn't matter, if it is good, you name it. I love all of those. Favourite games in the action RPG genre are TES III: Morrowind, TES V: Skyrim, and Sekiro: Shadows Die Twice. In the genre of story telling RPGs Dragon Age Origins, Gothic  and  plethora of D&D (Baldur's gate 2, Neverwinter Nights, etc.).

I invested 40 hours into the game. Some triple A titles don't give you even that, or you are 2/3rds done at the 40th hour mark, while here I am not sure how much I have even scratched the surface. However, if there are more core gameplay elements, which have not been revealed by the 40th hour, this would simply be bad, so I assume I have a fairly good grasp of what kind of  game this is. I have tried to immerse myself into the Witcher world a fair bit. I've read all the novels and also have watched the Netflix before, so getting the game finally (given my background in RPGs), was inevitable. However, I just don't get hooked and I think I know now why - it is simply not a role-playing game. Well, it is, as long as you want to role-play as Geralt of Rivia. 

[b] The good: story [/b]
The WItcher 3 is an amazing story-driven game with wonderful graphics even for 2020 (came out 5 years ago). It is probably one of the most beautiful games ever made (to date). It has great writing and even the smallest quests feel meaningful and definitely don't feel like autogerenated bullshit ("The Jarl left some bounty on some bandits" ring a bell to anyone?) The characters have a deep and rich history as well as really interesting interactions. If you've played earlier games it would be even better.

You can play the game simply for the story. Quests and character writing is amazing. An example of a great storyline in the beginning is the Baron questline. Simply wonderful.


[b] The good: gameplay elements [/b]
The game has some great gameplay elements. Fast Travel is neither forbidden nor prevalent, it works through access points, of which there are enoguh. A lot is streamlined such as 

[b] The good: visuals and sound [/b]
Music and voice acting is top notch. Really helps with immersion. Music especially. I wanted to get in battle solely to listen to the battle music. Oh how I hate typically the battle music in most games. Graphics is also perfect.


[b] The bad: role play [/b]
The biggest pet peeve I have with the game is that it doesn't give you much freedom how you want to play or what you want to do. 

You are Geralt, a pretty well defined badass, who either does stuff for money, or for less money (if you choose not to haggle) and is adored by a lot of hot women.

The game gives you pretty much two playstyles:
Do you want to be a knight in shining armor, showing compassion, being up-and-personal with two-handed sword? You can. Do you want to be a not-so nice knight, who prefers not to talk his way out of it but to use two-handed swords to go through life? You can. [b] This is pretty much it.[/b] Want to be a badass glass-cannon mage who cares only about knowledge and does forbidden stuff? Forget about it. A thief, sneaking around, backstabbing people? Nah. A ranger who doesn't like settlements and is one with nature? Nope. Yes, if you want you can use "spells" or alchemy to enhance your play, but in any case, you only enhance the sword play. You can also use a crossbow. 

Don't get me wrong, story-wise there are choices in this game and they do matter but the role-playing elements are really limited. It is hard to explain, in Gothic for example, you also play with a specific character with background but it doesn't feel nearly as restricted.


[b] The bad: loot [/b]
Finding that amazing piece of gear is a huge spike in dopamine. If you've ever played Diablo 2 (with loot randomization) or Morrowind (with fixed items), you know what I am talking about. At least up to the 16 th level that I got to (40 hrs of gameplay), loot feels really underwhelming. The worst part was, when at level 11 I decided to do a Witcher  contract in Novigrad rated for level 15 hoping that I would get some nice thing and I got a lowsey sword that was for characters of level 7. The quest involved a certain someone which was killing guards, some hooves and a brothel :)). 

The game has a heavy focus on crafting and collecting resources for that, which was done in the year 2001 in Morrowind. Since then, every game has it, becoming more and more tedious the grind to collect and make new gear.

[b] The bad: action [/b] 
For an aciton RPG I don't like the combat at all. Feels clucky, Geralt is a ballet dancer rotating needlessly. Most of the game is dodge, hit or roll-hit (for bigger monsters). The Witcher contracts are more interesting, where you get some special monsters, but the combat is quite repetitive without much input for the user. Maybe I am spoiled, because lastly I played Sekiro: Shadow DIe Twice ( a 2019 game) and I will probably never play a game with such an amazing action sequences- blocking, parrying , dodging feels ALIVE when you play it. If you like how combat feels in Skyrim or Sekiro, you will NOT like this one.

[b] The bad: skill system  [/b] 
The skill system is really weird and unnatural. You choose your skills, but you have to "activate" them, even when many of them are simply passive. I got to level 8 without any of them wihtout knowing. If you get used to the combat quickly (dodge and hit, every-single-time), you don't need them at lower difficulty levels. If you get a specific upgrade, you unlock the "jedi mind trick" that lets you bypass a lot in quests, by simply getting what you want with a swipe of the hand (literally).


[b] Verdict: [/b]
Overall, the game, for me, was totally overrated. It is not a traditional RPG in any case. This is not a  RPG in the traditional sense. It is a hack-and-slash action game. Yes, choices matter and the writing is great and worth playing for, if you only want narrative. But even that gets boring at some point. Here is how contracts work: go to the "crime scene", click on the right-mouse button, follow the trail to the lair, kill the monster (the fun  part), collect payment. Overall the game is okay.

Still not done with the main quest, but I give this game an 8.5 out of 10 and here's why:

tldr at the bottom...

Graphically, the game is absolutely breathtaking.  The environments, character models, cutscenes, and rendering is superb.  Sometimes while playing the game, I just have to pause and look around at the mountains, forests, and seas and marvel.  

Gameplay and combat feels a lot smoother than The Witcher 2.  Playing with a mouse and keyboard is truly where the controls shine.  I originally started playing with a controller, but I had to ditch it after a couple of minutes.  The game needs to be played with the mouse and keyboard.  

Controls can be really overwhelming though.  I originally began playing this game during college when it first released, but classes and work hindered me from really enjoying the game, so I had to put it down for weeks at a time.  Giving me plenty of time to forget what the control scheme was.  Now that I have my own life and work, I used my 2-week vacation break to really dig into the game and give it a go.  That's how it deserves to be played.  Unlike Skyrim where I can play it for 30 minutes, set it down and play it later, this game usually requires my attention for a couple of hours a day if not more.  

The story and side quests are amazing thus far.  Well written and tugs on your heart-strings in some instances.  Like I mentioned, at this time I haven't completed the main quest, but it's still really enjoyable.  The amount of choices you can make is wonderful, and sometimes you have to make split-second decisions in dialog.  

Lastly is soundtrack and sound design.  I could listen to the music in this game for days on end.  The last time I felt that strongly about a game's soundtrack was Darksiders: II.  I really enjoy the music and it doesn't bother me like Skyrim's did at one time (it was just a phase.  I grew out of it.).  As for the sound design, excellent.  You really feel like a Witcher with the various sounds you hear coming from the woods or off the trails.  Wander around outside of cities in the Wilderness and you'll get what I mean.  

The only cons I have are the glitches and bugs.  Sometimes cutscenes will begin and characters will perpetually be casting a noisy spell or have a weapon drawn or holding a random object but not really holding it (just sort of glued to the palm of their hand).  That can really take you out of the immersion.  Occasionally, a group of other npcs or animals will flock through your cutscene, blocking your view of characters and what's going on.  This happened to me while doing a rescue mission.  Whole herd of deer wandered into our little camp and just stood inside the characters while we talked around a campfire.  Sometimes characters clothes won't load, so there will be some floating heads and hands.  As long as this game has been out, you'd think they'd fix something like that (unless it was patched in the GotY edition).  

So that's my review.  As stated before 8.5/10.  Deducting 0.5 points for the vast amount of keybinds and control schemes you have to remember.  1.0 point for the glitches and bugs throughout the game.  Some of them are immersive breaking, but not game breaking.  

tldr: game's really good. 8.5/10. has some bugs and glitches that break immersion.  need to dedicate a lot of time to play it, or else you might forget controls.  that's it. see ya.

With almost 300 hours played, and having played the original game and the 2 expansions (Hearts of Stone and Blood & Wine), I feel now it's the time for my Witcher 3 review:

It's simply the best open world RPG I have ever played, and it will be hard to top it. 
- The main arc is a well elaborated and powerful story with plenty of choices that can determine how you navigate through the game and which ending you achieve.
- The two expansions are gold, especially Blood & Wine. They are definitely worth playing. And on a personal note, Blood & Wine seems the perfect ending to the Witcher saga.
- Bang for your buck: If you are a completionist you would probably spend at least 150 hours playing. If you get to purchase the game + 2 expansions for 15 EUR or less that means each hour of gameplay is 0.1 EUR. 
- If you finish the game and still want more you have the option to play the game again, but starting with the level, skills and inventory you finished the game with. There is a couple new additions to the skill tree that make this second go even more rewarding because now you play 100% of the game with the skills of a proper Witcher.
- Character development is top notch. Every character has its motivations and they are consistent throughout the game
- Geralt's skill tree is very broad so you can have different experiences of playing depening on how you choose to assign your skill points when levelling up.  
- The graphics are amazing and the game does not feel old. You can play it in the highest resolutions and still be amazed at the level of detail, the light effects...
- The soundtrack is immersive. With a nice headset you will feel you are in the game and will lose track of time.
- Gwent: it has one of the best games within a game ever designed. Playing Gwent is so fun that you might travel just to play a couple of card games against worthy rivals.

To sum up: if you like open world RPG: buy it. If you don't: buy it too. If the game + expansions are on sale for 15 EUR or below don't hesitate.

Excellent role-playing game. Deeply rich story and setting, bordering the overwhelming. The world feels alive, and the arc of feeling overwhelmed by to opponents to hunting down every single monster is very satisfying. I felt like combat wasn't as varied as it was in other games, but it's not a cakewalk by any means, and tackling challenges well above your level by proper preparation with oils and potions feels very thematic. Hunting down recipes for crafting these upgrades is then likewise a very rewarding thing, and gives you real incentive to explore this beautifully crafted world.

There's a reason CD Project Red acquired such high status, and this illustrates that reason perfectly. There's no need to have played the prior two witcher games (they're nowhere near as good), but if you haven't, you're going to want to pay attention to the exposition.

Play this game. The only true RPGs I had played before were from Bethesda. This game is completely different. The story is compelling and captivating, the combat is engaging and fun, and quests are mysterious and consequential. As if that was not enough, the game runs as well as I could ever ask. Does it have bugs? Yes, a few annoying ones for people with rebound controls. Do I really care? Not really, because the game is just that good.

With that said the game is beautiful and Im sure if I persisted with it I'd end up looking past the badly formed combat system, boring crafting and character progression. Безусловно одна из лучших и интереснейших игр. Если дать этой игре графику 21 года, то она встанет на 1 месте, обогнав по качеству киберпанк и другие топовые игры. 
Большое количество квестов, большие локации. Играть можно очень долго. Графика отличнейшая, текстуры очень четкие, нет мыла. Все очень просто, но при этом красиво и приятно. 
Каждая игровая локация имеет свои особенности. Например в велене мрачная погода и почти постоянно идет дождь. 
В общем игра классная, а если учитывать, что каждую распродажу она продается со всеми дополнениями всего за 450 рублей, то это вообще подарок судьбы. Однозначно стоит купить.

Amazing game. Despite what most people say, I think that reading the books (and at least watching recap videos for the first two games) makes a huge difference in enjoyment. The game IS a story-based game after all, and so recognising places, stories, and people makes the world infinitely more immersive and exciting to explore. This is a huge time dump, but I really believe that if you want to get the full experience of the game you should just commit.

The game itself is not perfect, of course, there are some bugs, but nothing game-breaking that I experienced. My only real problem with the game is that there is an unexpected "bad ending" that you can get and it's night and day levels of bad. This fact by itself is fine but the problem comes from a combination of the game not warning you and the fact that the factors that determine what ending you get aren't clear. Most other games give you a bad ending if you choose to be a villain or kill too many people. In this game, though, it entirely comes down to [spoiler] what dialogue options you say to Ciri. Unfortunately, the "correct" option isn't always clear. If you're unlucky like me, it can come down to whether or not you accompanied her into a room of sorceresses or a throwaway line about telling her that she doesn't need to be the best at everything. [/spoiler] My issue with this is how vastly different the endings are. [spoiler] In one, Ciri dies and Geralt supposedly commits suicide, which also renders useless any romancing of Triss or Yennefer that you did. In the other, Ciri survives and Geralt spends the rest of his days happily with whoever you choose to romance. Keep in mind that in my playthrough my bad ending came down to the fact that I chose to accompany her into the room of sorceresses which apparently caused her to not be independent enough to survive the last part of the game. [/spoiler] There are 5 decisions that determine the ending but out of the hundreds of times you talk to people you don't know which ones are it. What's more is that you can get the bad ending without even trying to be bad. You can accidentally get the bad ending for coddling her just a little too much OR by being an asshole. It sucks to play for so long just for everyone to die but so long as you either don’t care or are aware of the endings before you start, the game otherwise is 100% worth playing.

장점은 수도 없이 언급되었을테니 단점만 좀 적어보자면

1> 조작감이 정말 뭐같다. 특히 로치(말)의 조작감과 물속에서의 조작감이 정말 구리다. 내가 운전 배울때 몰아본 10만 km 넘게 탄 아토즈보다 조종성이 더 떨어진다. 물속에서도 마찬가지....스쿠버 처음 배울 때 느낌이 들 정도다.

2> 버그 많다. 물론 오픈월드에 버그는 꼭 따라다니는 거라고들 하는데 어크와 비교할 때 버그 상당히 많다. 빠른 저장 습관을 들이는 게 좋다.

3> 그래픽이 미묘하게 부족하다. 현실성이 좀 떨어지는 느낌이라고 해야 하나. 

4> 전투는 호불호가 갈리는데 액션 게임이라기 보다는 가위 바위 보 하는 기분이다. 쿠엔 만능이기도 하고

다른 게임이라면  이런 단점들 때문에 구매를 심각하게 고민해야만 한다. 하지만 위쳐3다. 역대 최고의 게임중 하나이며 최다 GOTY다.  그래서 이런 단점 따위는 하챦다.

경치구경하면서 돌아다니는 것만으로도 힐링이 되는 게임... 
언젠가부터 일상 속 산책하듯이 위쳐3 플레이하게 된다.

초반에는 지루했는데, 하다보면 돌아다니면서 퀘스트를 진행하는 것 만으로도 점점 몰입감이 높아진다. 서브퀘스트까지 이렇게 몰입감을 주는 스토리를 가진 게임은 드물다. 그리고 이게 정말 2015년도에 출시된 게임의 그래픽인가 싶을 정도로 그래픽이 멋지다. 호라이즌 제로던을 플레이할때 받았던 그래픽의 감동을 이게임에서도 받을 수 있었다. 추가 모드인 HD Reworks Project를 설치한다면, 성능저하 없이 디테일을 극한으로 올린 화면을 즐길 수 있다.

위처3 플레이를 망설이는 이유중에서 '1과 2를 플레이를 해야하는가'가
많은 사람들이 궁금해하는 부분일텐데.. 그래서 나역시 1,2 까지 다 샀다;;

결론부터 말하자면, 게임위쳐시리즈는 소설 이후의 스토리를 다루고 있다. 1과 2에서는 게롤트가 기억을 잃고 일어났던 일을다루고, 3부터 게롤트가 다시 기억을 되찾고 나서 시작되기 때문에 소설과 스토리상 바로 이어지는건 3다. (기억을 잃었던 동안의 에피소드나 인물과의 관계에 1, 2의 영향이 어느정도 있긴 하지만 크게 중요하진 않은 것 같다.) 

따라서, 1과 2를 꼭 플레이를 할 필요는 없다고 생각한다. (기억을 잃었던 기간의 스토리는 요약영상을 보는 것만으로도 충분) 1,2를 잘 몰라도 3를 플레이 하는데 거의 지장없고, 3를 플레이하다보면 중간중간 이전 줄거리들을 설명 해주거나 대화 선택지에서 물어볼 수도 있다. (좀 길어지긴 하겠지만..)

위쳐3를 플레이할지 고민하는 시간에 위쳐3를 바로 플레이하는게 훨씬 낫다. 이전작은 위쳐3를 다 하고 정 할게 없을때 기억을 잃었을때 일어났던 일들이 너무 궁금하고 더 자세히 알고 싶다면, 그때 플레이해보는 것도 나쁘지 않다고 생각한다. (실제로 나도 위쳐3 본편 엔딩을 다 봤고 DLC를 플레이중인데 다 하고나면 2를 플레이할 예정이다.)

최신 게임과 비교해도 전혀 손색없는 그래픽... 모드설치하면 더 향상이 되버리는 클라스.. 압도적인 스토리와 몰입감.. 2020년 현 시첨에서도 위쳐3를 능가할 만한 게임은 아직 없는 것 같다. 위쳐3를 접한 이후로 다른 게임들은 손도 못대고 있다. 그냥 이 게임이 일상이 되어버렸고, 조금씩 그 스토리에 빠져들면서 이젠 재미여부를 떠나서 위쳐 세계를 돌아다니는게 산책이고 일상이 되어버는 것 같다.

It's a bloody awesome game, especially after reading the books.

- Great stories, setting, monsters, and lore.
- Tons of mods that can improve the game.
- The combat is the weakest part of the game. It is a combination of choreography and hitbox system, which in this case resulted in an overall shallow system.
- AI is bad and has the old problem found in this type of games, where enemies are confined to an area that they cannot leave. So, you can easily bait them at the edge of that area and just engage them at your leisure.
- You can get over leveled and find combat unchallenging. I tried fixing this with a popular mod that un-levels the world. With it, the game was challenging for a good while, but inevitably, it also became generally easy down the road. Also, you wouldn’t really need to change your armor or weapon since many items were very similar, which is part of the fun in an RPG. I also tried the very popular overhaul, Ghost Mode, which changes almost everything. With it the game became (much) harder, and thus making it better.

Gra fantastyczna! Wciągająca fabuła, niesamowita grafika, pięknie odwzorowany świat, ponury klimat jak w książkach Sapkowskiego, świetnie wyreżyserowane postacie... Czego by tu jeszcze nie pisać!
Genialne jest to, że to my mamy kontrolę nad fabułą, to co się potem stanie, los innych postaci jest w naszych rękach. To my jesteśmy odpowiedzialni za to, czy będziemy dobrzy dla ludzi czy bezwzględni i okrutni. Mamy władzę nad losem świata. Uratujemy czy doszczętnie go zrujnujemy.
Niesamowicie rozbudowany system walki. To od nas zależy czy walczymy ofensywnie, czy stawiamy na defensywę. Mutacje, w dodatku "Krew i wino", dodają nam świetne umiejętności umożliwiające np. dłuższe przeżycie.
Dużo różnych eliksirów i odwarów z bestii umożliwią nam mnóstwo różnych bonusów, które na wyższych poziomach trudności są absolutnie obowiązkowe.
Aktorzy głosowi świetnie dopasowani do swoich postaci, bardzo dobrze oddają charakter postaci i ich emocji. I stąd chciałbym serdecznie pozdrowić moją znajomą Annę Terpiłowską grającą postać Priscilli, która świetnie zaśpiewała "Wilczą zamieć".
Apropos muzyki. Pięknie oddająca klimat średniowiecza i sytuacji związanych z akcją gry. Muzyka porusza tak samo mocno jak sama historia Wiedźmina 3. Po prostu zakochałem się w tej muzyce.
Podsumowując, warto zagrać, choćby nawet ze względu na samą genialność tej gry, klimat, muzykę, historię i piękne widoki. Weźcie również pod uwagę, że ta gra ma już na karku ponad 5 lat, a nawet 6. I nadal bardzo dobrze wygląda!
Polecam bardzo serdecznie!

I put off playing this game for years, until I had a good enough box to play it, and just finished it. I won't write a long review, but will say that this is the richest open world fantasy RPG ever made. Red Dead 2 is the only game that is equivalent, and both are shining examples of how to produce a game as art. Anyone who has not played this amazing game needs to pick it up and play through it - get the DLCs, too, they are amazing as well. Great ending, better than anything you'd find in any TV series. Only a well written series of novels would give you the same experience. Look, just get it and be done with it if you like well-done fantasy worlds or like solid action RPGs.

Don't be put off by how old The Witcher 3 is - It's still a great game that is just so much fun all of the time, and the graphics hold up really well and are amazing. The main storyline is thorough and enjoyable and there are loads of side quests to enjoy. The map is also huge, but not so big that I wouldn't recognise where I was. I found myself completing almost all the side quests not to level up, but just because they're all a lot of fun. The only criticism would be how the side quests mostly serve as a distraction from the main story rather than complementing it. There's no need to rush though - take your time and enjoy all of the side quests! The combat is also simplistic but surprisingly good.

Overall, The Witcher 3 is still an amazing game today, and is absolutely worth the price. If it's also on sale, then go for it - you won't be disappointed

Deeply immersive storytelling and gameplay. Unlike Elder Scrolls and some other series, this game has a sense of urgency and purpose surrounding the main story, keeping it from getting lost in the noise of sidequests and monster contracts. Some of the sidequests dovetail perfectly with the main story, giving you the option to either push ahead as quickly as possible or take care of interesting additional story along the way. Alchemy, crafting, and skill trees have many deep elements and details that can take a long time to master, but fortunately they're not critical, so you can do so at your own pace.

여태껏 한 게임이 그닥 많지 않아서 뭐라 표현해야 할지 모르겠다.
그치만 평가가 압도적으로 긍정적, 이거 하나만 보고 믿어도 된다. 
굳이 까놓고 말하면 제일 큰 문제가 전투라 할만큼 전투 부분은 자기가 제대로 연구해서 표식 위쳐 같은 거 하지 않는 이상은
대부분 쿠엔 표식 떡칠이나 아니면 반격, ALT 회피 정도? 즐길거리는 3개가 끝일거다.
전투가 좀 단조롭다는 거 하나만 제외하면 모든 부분은 진짜 완벽하다.
그래픽은 말할 것도 없거니와, CDPR 얘네가 세부설정 제대로 짜놓고 게임 만든 티가 팍팍 난다.
스토리도 처음엔 뭔 소린지 모르지만 다 깨고 곱씹어 생각할 수 있을 정도로 깊이 있다.
제발 부탁이니까 처음 몇 시간 해놓고 라이브러리에 처박아두지 말고 '피의 남작' 퀘스트 한 번 깨고나서 해석본 하나 정독하길 바란다. 이게 이런 의미였구나. 내가 한 선택이 이런 결과를 초래했구나. 생각하면 진짜 그 순간이 빠져드는 순간일테니까.

Pro:
Each quest and NPC has it's own story which is amazing.
Artwork and soundtrack are great.
Plenty of builds you can go for and items you can use.
Really enjoyed the horse racing events, brawler and gwent games.
Con:
The combat mechanics would have greatly benefited from some polishing.
Terrain and object interaction could have been better (especially horse riding).
A handful of bugs left behind that brake a bit the immersion of the game.
Some overpowered enemies (eg. caretaker).
The enchantment crafting is way to expensive when compared with your income.
The huge number of point of interest in skellige isles is just boring to clear.
The grand master-crafted sets and the extended talent tree are available too late in the game.

Overall, a very good game with a great story and good role-playing experience.
Would definitely play again and recommend to other players.

Когда я купил её на распродаже вместе со всеми дополнениями за тысячу рублей, хайп по этой игре уже давно прошел. Но самое интересное то что установив её и начав играть я удалил её почти сразу, не увидев для себя рпг составляющей, как выбор персонажа и его создание, а мне сразу подсунули седого мужика с предысторией которой я не знал и изучать не хотел ибо ленив. Спустя год или два, у меня на работе появился коллега который только и трындел о Ведьмаке, а так же я услышал положительный отзыв от старого друга которого не видел около 3х лет, когда был у него в гостях в Новосибирске. Ну и наткнулся на пару роликов на ютуб. Ииии понеслось все с фразы Геральта "Никак вы блядь не научитесь!", это просто ахуенная игра! Прошел 2 раза обычный и на на игре +, вместе со всеми ДЛЦ. И наверняка пройду еще раз!
Очень крутой сюжет и все остальное, не буду даже писать огромный обзор, просто скажу что игра шедевр.

[h1]Why the Witcher 3 will forever join the realm of legendary games[/h1] [b] Or how I spent 3 years of my life getting halfway through the Witcher 3[/b] [h2] Little Back Story between Geralt, The Witcher and I. [/h2] I've been playing the Witcher 3 for a few years now. I have almost 80 hours in. I've jumped in and out of this game over the year but now I am determined to beat it. I looked up how long it usually takes to beat and I've found ranges from 60-200+ hours. I am only nearing the halfway point of the main story line. There is a lot to do in this game. [h2]Constantly Crazy Cinematics, Comprende? [/h2] Spires in the distance, frosted mountain peaks to the South. Forest upon evergreen forest just looking as real and as lush as the Amazon jungle. I've actually had to [b]stop in my tracks[/b] many times in this game and just take a minute to appreciate the [b] MOST STUNNING VISUALS I'VE SEEN IN A GAME.[/b] I actually bought this specific game because I ended up with a 1080ti GPU and wanted to see just how good the best looking game can look. It's good. Damn good. Better than good. It's stunning. [h2]Combat yet to be equalled in a modern Action RPG,[/h2] I feel like the Witcher 3 really captures the balance between too many options during combat and too few. I don't like games that feature and overwhelming amount of options for every single attack or defense. The Witcher 3 has just the right amount of combat features. You can do fast or strong attacks, you can hold shift to alter them. There is 'magic' but only a few 'spells' to choose from. They aren't called magig though, they are called signs and they make the game a lot more exciting. Defense consists hitting space and rolling out of the way, hitting alt and dodging and parrying with a right click (I don't think I've parried one attack but I'm not playing on the most difficult setting. [h2] QUESTS, so many QUESTS[/h2] This is why I got so far behind the game in the first place. The developers did an outstanding job of making the quests immersive. Gone are the days of travelling to a dungeon to defeat a few Draugers to get someone their fathers sword back (Skyrim reference). The side quests often interlace with the main quest, and all the side quests are unique and the rewards are always a surprise. You might spend 2 hours on a quest only to get a small amount of experience, or you might beat one in 15 minutes and go up a level. [b]Con about side quests:[/b] The option for enemy scaling always makes the older, lower level quests a bit more challenging, but I find the amount of experience is always nearly negligible for a lower level quest. So much so that now I am racing through the main quest so I can get to some harder quests. You do run the risk of ending up like me, [b]80 hours in and only halfway done.[/b] You can really go at your own pace in this game. I love to sit down for a few hours and really have a session, but the quests are always broken down into bite size chunks - you can get something done even if you have 15 minutes to play. I never liked those games that feel like you really have to put in a time commitment if you want to get anywhere. [b]Not so with Geralt and the Crew,[/b] [h2] Why You Should Buy the Witcher 3[/h2] Immersive, amazing graphics, hardly any bugs, smooth gameplay, great storyline, you can style Geralts hair and beard, you can really [b]give the bad guys a piece of your mind with some of the chat options. So many monsters, so much alchemy, great collection of weapons and armour, you get to feel like you are [b]adventuring through the world seeking out Wraiths and Alghouls rather than chill in your house waiting for covid to go away. [h3]PumpkinMumpkins final verdict: [b]Must Buy[/b][/h3] Forget about the toilet paper, forget about food for a few days, forget about what ever you have to forget about to get this game in your collection. Thank you for taking the time to read my second ever review. If you liked it please like and follow. Have a great day!

[h1]Overview a.k.a. TL, DR:[/h1]

Played many medieval/fantasy-themed games in the past, whether it's RPG, strategy, or action-RPG, and I'm just 'dived' into The Witcher franchise recently because my PC finally good enough to play it properly. I have to say, this game in particular are one of the games that actually got me excited for its storyline alone. Never got this (wholeheartedly) hooked before. Great!

[h1]The Good[/h1]

[list]
[*] Beautiful visuals, still holds up even today
[*] Huge enough game world for an RPG
[*] Believable story, even for someone who doesn't indulge in medieval fantasy and preffered Sci-Fi for an RPG game
[*] The HUD are fairly crowded, but not intrusive enough for me
[*] Expansive crafting and character development system
[*] The music and sounds in general.
[*] Controls are fairly easy to comprehend 
[*] Supportive elements of the games like minigames or other activities in game doesn't really interfere with the story
[*] Expansive graphical settings and post-processing like AA actually not that taxing in this game, unlike others
[*] Actually made me save scumming to get an all good ending on my first playthrough, but the save file are fairly small unlike Fallout/Skyrim/Football Manager
[*] Could actually bully town guard without fear of getting hanged afterwards
[*] Racial prosecution simulator™
[*] Gwent
[*] Geralt's drawing skills
[*] The fair maids of the northern realms ( ͡° ͜ʖ ͡°)
[/list]

[h1]The Bad and the Ugly[/h1]

[list]
[*] I know it's Geralt's story, but If Geralt actually got an amnesia or something like in the first game (?) RPG perfectionists could have more freedom to develop the character and Geralt not knowing a lot of things already at the start could be more believeable[spoiler]like, a lot more creature should be listed in bestiary already, or a lot more crafting/alchemy diagrams should already exist at the start.[/spoiler]
[*] Many aspects should be in the vanilla game, like all quest pins should appear in world map (yes this have been mitigated with mods, but some people just wanna play and enjoy the game)
[*] NPC faces (at least that we can interact with like merchants) are really similiar and not enough variety 
[*] In a dream world, there shouldn't be any leveling system on this game, only progressed through the story and gain/earn abilities like Skyrim (do more to get better), can't stand Geralt got bullied by mere Drowners at early game. I don't know, I might be a dimwit at fighting Drowners.
[*] Dammit even 100 hours of gameplay not enough to find more dirt on this game lmao, yeah it's that good
[/list]

[h1]10/10 would save scum for this game[/h1]

Look at 2015 and name one game that stood the test of time and still looks like it could top the charts if it were sold now as it was released then. Yes, only one comes to mind: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt.

Straight up the best game I've ever played, here's some of the good and bad I experienced.

PROS:
LONG game time with expansions, Story-rich, good combat system, lots of decision-making to affect the game from start to finish, AWESOME graphics, great music, miles of land to explore, a plethora of enemies to fight everywhere, great upgrade system where you can create many different custom character builds, lots of online support to help you on the path to name a few.

CONS:
Sometimes clunky and still has the occasional bug, 'Roach' often doesn't work properly (not popping up on map when called, not moving from a location, getting stuck on everything) and have to restart a save to fix it, the enemies you fight generally are just re-skins, can sometimes be repetitive as all RPG's are.

I must say, although I did experience some cons in this game, in no way whatsoever did it distract me, or take anything away from my gaming experience which was absolutely awesome. I'm on my 3rd playthrough now, and it still hasn't gotten old.

Would have never bought this game if I wasn't gifted it because I played WItcher 1 and 2 and had a terrible time both of the times, but a good friend gave it to me and I think it's one of the best action adventures with RPG elements out there now.

The story has some points that upset me, the world is kind of simplistic, pessimistic and... boring in its presentation of a kinda low fantasy medieval place, and sometimes when you're made to make a choice, the consequences make absolutely no sense. A lot of the characters behave irrationally mostly to enforce gritty dark fantasy, and these things all bothered me.

But the atmosphere is great, Geralt is cool, the combat is fun, the whole Witcher lifestyle is presented very well. The landscape is beautiful, the areas are well-designed, and the different RPG elements are also kind of nice. I did spoiler myself on how to get the best ending because of course the way to get it is equally nonsensical, but I'm glad I did it, because I walked away with a positive impression of Witcher 3 for it.

위쳐3를 재밌다고 느끼기까지 정말 많은 시간이 걸렸습니다. 30시간 쯤 됐을 때 메인 스토리가 거의 막바지에 다다랐던것 같은데 그제서야 재밌어지더군요. 그 전에는 전투도 답답하고 스토리도 눈에 안들어오고 이렇게 지루한게 뭘 그렇게 사람들이 명작이라고 칭송을 하는지 모를 정도 였는데 말이죠.

본 편의 경우는 아마 전작과 제법 연관성이 짙은지 끝까지 해도 스토리를 전부 이해하기는 힘이 들었습니다. 그래서 재미를 빨리 못붙이지 않았나 싶기도 하구요. 반대로 DLC의 경우 딱히 메인스트림과 큰 연관이 없는 스토리라 몰입도 잘되고 정말 재밌었습니다.

전투의 경우는 사실 익숙해지는게 가장 어려웠던 부분이었습니다. 아무래도 몬스터헌터를 많이한 터라 모든 전투를 몬헌에 대입해서 생각하는 경우가 많은데 위쳐의 전투는 몬헌과는 정말 많이 달랐기에 초반엔 정말 힘들었네요. 하지만 익숙해지고나니 제법 속도감이 있고 가벼운 액션과 몬스터에 맞춰서 어떤 마법을 쓸지 고민하는게 재밌었습니다.

가장 중요한 부분이 NPC와의 상호작용이 아닐까 생각합니다. 정말 많은 NPC와 대화 및 서브퀘스트 등으로 상호작용을 하게 되는데 별거 아니라고 생각했던 선택지가 퀘스트가 끝날 떄 발목을 잡는다든지, 메인스트림의 판도가 바뀌어 버린다든지 등 특히 NPC와의 관계에 관련된 선택지는 신중하게 선택을 하게끔 하더군요. 물론 저는 그런걸 신경쓰지 않기 때문에 제법 고생도 했지만요. 이 부분은 선형적인 스토리 진행을 좋아하는 저로서는 오히려 싫어야 하는 부분이지만 이것 저것 많은 걸 느끼게 해주었고 결과적으로 게임의 재미를 끌어올려주지 않았나 생각합니다. 진행의 완성도는 차치하더라도요.

또 쓰다보니 이렇게 길어졌는데 아무튼 정말 재밌게 했고 근시일 내에 2회차를 하지 않을까 생각합니다. 이번엔 좀 더 신중하게 선택지를 골라야겠어요.

The game becomes uninteresting after a while because while well written you are essentially doing the same thing over and over again. The game play is simplistic in that you dodge, attack, and repeat. After repeating the same attacks for dozens of hours you get so good at it that the game becomes extremely easy. Levelling up takes a long time and the rewards are limited, and the magic is simple and not interesting to play with. The great graphics kept me captivated for a long time and the scenes are great to look at, but eventually the beauty of the game only captivates you for so long until you discover that every mission is the same; You go out and kill some enemies. I played for over 100 hours and slowly got tired of it until I decided to quit and play something else, and I don't really feel like going back to it now.

To get the negative bits out of the way, i found a few parts of the campaign a bit tedious/boring. Now on to the good parts, and for me the most important factor: This is a game filled with good writing. The entire world and all of its characters (main and side) are believable. You wouldn't think it makes such a difference, but i always hated it when a character acts differently to his established ideals. Here, this never happens. Your bond to the characters thus never diminishes. That doesn't mean that everyones black and white - most of the stories and characters are grey, with good and bad parts and it's tough to choose a side.

Of course gameplay, graphis, mechanics etc are all well and good, but i believe for me, the writing is what makes this game truly great and enjoyable.

Have fun :)

총평 : 
훌륭한 스토리와 밀도있는 환경으로 몰입하기 좋은 오픈월드 액션 RPG게임. 액션 RPG에 흥미가 있다면 구매하시는걸 강력히 추천. 세일을 많이 한다면 나중에 하더라도 구매하시는 것을 추천.

장점 
- 흥미롭고 몰입하기 쉬운 스토리
- 요구 사양에 비해 미려한 배경 그래픽
- 짜임새있고 밀도 높은 배경 및 환경 디테일
- 사소한 디테일도 신경을 써 몰입감이 한층 더 올라감(예:면도 후 천천히 자라는 주인공의 수염, 숲 속에서 들리는 다양한 새소리 등)
- 게임 내 미니게임인 궨트가 매우 재미있음.
- 모드를 지원하여 후술할 몇몇 단점을 개선가능함. 모드 설치 및 제거가 매우 간편함
- 나쁘지 않은 공식 한글화(자막만 지원)

단점
- 소울 시리즈와 비교하여 전투가 단순한 편.
- 특정 상황에 전투의 난이도가 크게 달라짐. 레벨 스케일링 옵션이 존재하지만 (기본값은 꺼짐) 그다지 합리적이지 않음.
- 퀘스트의 목적지를 위해 미니맵에 표시되는 네비게이션의 성능이 상당히 좋지 않음.
- 시스템과 UI가 불편함( 퀘스트 동시 추적 불가 등), 모드로 쉽게 개선 가능
- NPC의 얼굴 돌려쓰기가 심해서 가끔 몰입에 방해가 됨

After 200 hours, what can I say about this game? Such a remarkable, ambitious achievement of an RPG world that it really hasn't aged much at all. Still an absolute pleasure to explore and still home to some of the best writing and characters the genre's ever seen. For example when pluck an innocuous-sounding bounty from a village notice board, you never ever really know what kind of wild story you're about to get yourself tangled up in. Almost every quest feels weighty, accurate and worthwhile, with memorable stories inspired by some of the very strangest cuts from depths of Eastern European folklore(Thanks Andrzej Sapkowski).

This game is fantastic, it has an amazing story that i would rate 10 / 10. It is to this day my favourite game of all time, and a lot of factors contribute to that. One is for the character's, all the characters have a fantastic backstory and your decisions make a big impact on how you end the game. Another reason is the detailed contracts / side quests which i haven't seen in many other games. Every single contract and quest is different and not repetitive at all which is a common theme for some games such as Skyrim. And of course the graphics are fantastic in this game, even for a game that came out in 2015, it has astonishing graphics that could compete with some modern titles. I picked this game up on a sale not expecting much other than my friend saying it has good graphics and the story is good. I just thought it was gonna be another medieval game where the whole game is going through dungeons and grabbing an item for a character only to do that same thing again for another character, but it is so much more and i truly do recommend this game.

Now that I passed the 200 hours mark, I feel like I could give a proper review.
+ The environment is astounding
+ The story is richer than Jeff Bezos
+ The butterfly effect is actually a thing
+ The amount of abilities and enhancements is just insane
+ The music is almost always on point
+ The graphics, on a 144hz monitor are just gorgeous, despite being a 2015 release
+ Insane amount of side quests and possibilities to just "run around and kill stuff"
+ Option to save your progress at almost any given time, even in the middle of a mission
+ GWENT

- The quest rewards are relatively small (coin-wise)
- The items are very level-dependent, so even if you finish a quest with recommended character level of 20, whilest being level 13, you won't get a level 19/20/21 item, but a level 12/13/14/15 items
- The Wraiths
- Some glitches are still present (like ... you could literally get stuck somewhere), but on the bright side - those places are narrowed (at least based on my experience) to some places where you shan't be actually going. For the most part, no glitches are present

Overall, if you are into that genre and you are wondering whether the game is worth the money or not - it is. It absolutely is. The first day when I played it, I was playing for 16 hours straight ... my whole day off got sucked into Geralt's adventures.

I got this game on sale quite a while ago, and played through it last year, I wasn't sure what I was getting into since I'm not big on Witcher lore and the first two games felt kinda alienating to play for me. To my surprise, I ended up really liking and enjoying this game. Now I'm not really into this D&D/Medieval themed games or media usually and I don't usually like how austere, confusing, or uninvolving most RPGs feel, but this feels very much so playable unlike those games, and even though there's stuff that I still don't fully understand, for the most part it's either simple enough that you don't have trouble grasping concept or you come to understand how they work over time, and you can easily finish the game even without really understanding everything about them. Regardless, the story is fairly simple, you look for Ciri, the protagonist Geralt's figurative daughter and protege, who is on the run from an entity known as the Wild Hunt, as you look for clues and traces through three vast regions of swamp-infested Velen, urban centers of Oxenfurt and Novigrad, and the Nordic-like Isle of Skellige. There's even more with the DLC but the base game is expansive enough on it's own that it's kinda hard to run out of things to do, whether it be side quests, contracts, or treasures of some sort. Even though as I said I'm not big on Witcher lore you do end up becoming interested in the socio-economic and political situation of this world and your actions do define the ending as well as ending for various characters, and I should mention, it is vital to have interesting and well-written characters in relatively simple stories like these and well they nail that here too. Although Geralt is pretty lacking in his ability to display emotions due to his mutations, you still get some emotional moments and since he is the player's mouth-piece you do feel some relation to him, and everyone else, fellow witchers, Triss, Yennefer, Ciri, and others, are great characters on their own and there is a joy to interact with people overall. There is not much else to say here, great game.

The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt – ролевая игра нового поколения, действие которой разворачивается в удивительном фэнтезийном мире, где необходимо принимать сложные решения и отвечать за их последствия.

Мир охвачен войной. Армия империи Нильфгаард стирает все на своем пути, стремясь добраться до самого сердца Северных королевств. Движимый глубоко личными мотивами знаменитый ведьмак, Геральт из Ривии, не остановится ни перед чем, чтобы найти и защитить некую особу, о которой говорится в древнем пророчестве. Меж тем за ним самим по пятам следует враг, не ведающий страха и жалости, самый невообразимый кошмар – Дикая Охота, кавалькада призрачных всадников, предвестников рока и гибели, способных за одну лишь ночь не оставить и следа от целых поселений…

The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt предлагает нелинейную, взрослую, насыщенную историю и не имеющую аналогов боевую систему без использования QTE, которая сочетает широкий выбор тактических элементов, свойственных ролевым играм, с динамичными и зрелищными сражениями, достойными лучших игр жанра экшен.

First of all, the witcher 3 is a good game with some problems that keep it from being a perfect game.

The best thing without a doubt is the story, the characters and the setting, which have kept me intrigued and wanting to keep forward and explore everything. Also the soundtrack is very good.

The worst part is the gameplay. The Combat is quite smooth but there is no depth in it and all the fights feels the same. You can get over leveled very easy and find combat unchallenging, also there are too many potions, elixirs, oils and bombs which there is no need to use and using them only makes the combat even easier.
The skill tree is supposed to offer you a variety of possibilities to choose your build and playstyle but i feel the offensive branch is the best and it makes no sense to choose anything different, or at least in my experience i have not seen any need to do it.
Side quests are fine, but most have the same structure; talk to this guy, go to this place, kill the monster and come back for the reward. It is true that some quests are interesting and some of them have an unexpected twist but in general they are somewhat repetitive.

Absolutely incredible game you don't really need to play the previous games to understand the story which is one of the best stories i have ever seen, this game is the golden standard of a fantasy game with tons of creatures to fight, levels to grind, gear to obtain and level up, potions to make and spells to cast

Witcher 3 is one of the best games, if not the best, I have ever played. There's literally nothing wrong about it other than Roach, I hate Roach with a burning passion, however, even this mentally challenged horse will be great sometimes.

If you are like I was, and for some obscure reason you have not yet played this masterpiece, don't give it any second thoughts, just do it. To me this is a piece of entertainment art that will forever live as one of the best games in history. 

There's no reason not to play it and if you call yourself a gamer, you cannot not enjoy this gem.

So I got to the game five years late and it is still a blast. It feels like there is endless sidequests to find, monsters to fight and treasure to discover. The story is fun and doesn't really require much knowledge of the previous games to enjoy. Slight learning curve to the combat and inventory system but other than that it is pretty fantastic. With a huge modding community  that improve everything from the rain effects to the colour of an item, you'll not regret playing this one. Definitely on the best games ever and getting it on sale seems like a crime, one I'd commit again.

[h1]Beware: Witcher 3 is a Visual Novel[/h1]

At the end of the Prologue there is an event [spoiler]you have no choice but to kill some pitiful bandits at the Inn[/spoiler] which opened my eyes to the fact that this is primarily a Visual Novel and only secondarily an RPG. As for the roleplaying part, well, you're just along for the ride on Geralt's back. The choices you're given feel very limiting.

Edit: The game just forced me to play as another character [spoiler]Ciri[/spoiler]. No choice of abilities or skills. This is beyond awful! Some people call this "story driven", but that's deceptive. "Visual novel" is the proper label.

While it's not my favourite game of all time by a long shot I genuinely enjoyed the time I spend with the game. I grinded out all of the achievements, completed every quest I found and had my fun with the quality of life mods the nexus offers. So you could say I liked it.

I was happily suprised that some of my choices had consequences long after I had made them. The game does not cheese it's way through desicion making. You really have to think about your answer.

By the way play the game on the original voice sync. I played it in german and some of the dialog option were the exact opposite of what I wanted to say. All of a sudden I pissed of a character that I wanted to befriend. Really sucks in a game that is 85% Dialog. But that just as a sidenote.

I played the game on deathmarch from start to finish and was a little bit disappointed by the difficulty. I was expecting way more deaths than actually occured during my playthrough.

However I really enjoyed the gear system. Although there was less focus on giving me a chance to display my stuff than I would have wanted.

The game gives you all of this cool armor and weapons and then you only get the player-home in the secound DLC and there these armor racks that can only hold specific kinds of armorpieces. urgh...

The combat is alright. And thats about it. Kind of repetitive after a couple of hours but still engaging enough to keep me hooked somehow. Probably because it reminds me of Assassins Creed a little bit.

Half of the work is done by the soundtrack which I could not get enough of during the battles. Sometimes I deliberately stopped attacking and only evaded my enemy so I could strike them matching to the Battle OST. For me this is almost the best part about this game second only to the world building.

CDPR knows how to make you invested in a Gameworld. I especially fell in love with skellige, it's OST, the clan rivalries, it's map design, the unique enemys compared to the rest of the main game and the characters.

All in all I'd say if the game is on a 50% discount it's a buy for most people that want to have an engaging story. If I could change one thing about the Witcher 3 I probably would tie up some loose ends the story left me with.

I don't want to spoil anything. Just be wary that not every quest has a satisfying ending. Just because sometimes it does not have a proper ending at all. That goes for one main quest and several side quests in my opinion.

It's a good game with some missed opportunitys. Would buy again if I was given the choice. I got it in a 50% sale so 25€ for the Game of the Year edition.

To wrap this up my score is a 7/10.

The witcher 3 is a game that appeals to players who want a truly immersive story experience in which you really do feel like your decisions matter. You feel for characters and depending on how you want YOUR story to unfold, you really care about the decisions you make. Not once did I feel like a decision I made in the game was irrelevant or for no purpose. In addition to this amazing role playing aspect of the game, there is so much customization both gameplay and aesthetic wise with mutations, gear, cosmetics and skills. Geralt is also a character I absolutely loved in this game and in general all of the voice acting and story telling is really top notch from CD project red here. The graphics are absolutely stunning and all of the dlc is on point. Finally, the combat is super enjoyable aswell especially if you love being able to absolutely clap your enemies, sending heads, legs, arms and torsoes flying with your blades and crossbow or evaporating these scrubs with nutty magic.
All in all 10/10 game

재밌다 DLC까지 다 구입해서 해야 진짜 즐길 수 있다고 자부할 수 있다.

1. 게임성
이 게임의 특징은 선택의 중요성이 일단 있다. 내가 대화 중에 어떤 선택을 하느냐에 따라 지금 눈앞에 있는 녀석이 죽을 수도 있고 살아 남을 수도 있고 나에게 감사할지, 나에게 분노할지 그것마저 내 선택에 따라 갈린다.
이 게임에서 액션을 추구하는 사람들에게 말하자면 우리가 흔히하는 온라인 게임에서 나오는 엄청난 이펙트, 그리고 여기저기 쾅쾅 터지는 그런 것은 기대하기 힘들다. 기본적으로 괴물에게는 은검, 맹수나 사람에게는 강철검을 이용해서 회피를 하면서 기본 공격으로 적을 무찌르고 아주 기본적인 불길, 장풍, 기절, 방어막, 마법함정으로 적을 제압한다. 그러니까 막 거대한 검이 나타나서 적을 쓸어버리는 건 없다는 것이다. 정말 기본적인 정통RPG를 좋아하면 하자.(난 회피액션을 좋아해서 샀다.)

2. 조작감
키보드 조작이 괜찮다. 특별하게 더 편하다라는 건 아니지만 기본적인 게임을 하는데 아무런 문제가 없이 플레이 한다. 나 같은 경우는 Xbox조이스틱으로 했는데도 진짜 편했다. 조작을 하는데에 있어서 어려움을 느낄 일은 단 한번도 없었다.
하지만, 주인공 게롤트가 가끔 괴랄맞은 움직임을 보일때가 있다.(아주 가끔이다.) 이게 이제 물리엔진, 관성의 법칙을 이용하다보니까 주인공이 달리다가 바로 딱 멈추는게 아니라 몇걸음 더 가서 멈춘다. 그러다보니까 벼랑에서 딱 멈추려고 했는데 인식이 이상했는지 벼랑 아래로 점프한다. 바닥이면 죽고 물이면 다이빙한다. 이런 점만 뺀다면 조작감은 굉장히 괜찮다.

3.-1 게롤트의 하체
일단 한가지 단점을 말하자면 게롤트(주인공)의 하체는 보기와 다르다. 하체가 매우 단단한데다가 위쳐라는 특수한 신체를 가지고 있으니까 뭐 어디서 굴러도 살아남을 것 같지만 실제 사람이 떨어졌을 때 운이 안좋으면 다리가 부러질 것 같은 높이에서 게롤트는 사망한다. 죽는 높이가 엄청 낮다는 것이다. 그래서 좀 높긴한데 이정도면 떨어져도 살겠지 싶은 높이라면 일단 돌아서 가는 방법을 생각해보자.

3-2 너무나도 많은 콘텐츠
이 게임은 즐길거리가 굉장히 많다. 궨트는 말할 것도 없고 경마에다가 축제를 즐기는 것까지 구현했다. 거기다가 맵에 물음표가 엄청나게 많다. 근데 이것들은 다 하다보면 알 수 있지만 그냥 여기저기 돌아다니다가 아무것도 없는 길에서 갑자기 퀘스트가 출현할 때가 있다. 근데 한국 사람의 특성상 빠른 이동이 가능하면 빠른 이동을 이용해서 순간이동을 한다. 굳이 긴 거리를 말이나 (허약한) 두 다리로 이동하지 않는단 말이다. 그러다보니 모든 퀘스트를 클리어하고 싶은 사람들에게 이 점은 불편한 점으로 다가올 수도 있다.

3-3 후방주의!
이 게임에는 히로인 게롤트를 사랑하거나 사랑했던 사람들이 있다. 게임을 하다보면 피할 수 없는 장면이 나온다. 그래, 후방주의 말이다. 진짜로다. 여성의 하반신까진 안보여도 상반신 나체는 볼 수 있으며 여자가 이상한 소리를 내며 앞뒤로 움직이는 장면을 보고 아 얘내가 뭐하는지 알겠구나 싶은 장면이 진짜로 있다. 심지어 가끔 맵에 하트 표시가 있는게 있는데 그거 바로 돈내고 하는 것이다. 이 게임으로 스트리밍을 할 생각이라면 일단 접어두기로 하고 생각을 하자.(트위치에서는 하는 거 같긴 하던데)

3-4 위쳐3
이 게임은 위쳐3다. 말 그대로 세번째 작품이다. 1과 2의 후속작이란 말이다. 게임을 하다보면 2에서 일어났던 일을 언급하기도 하는데 3가 처음 해보는 작품이라면 고개를 갸웃거리는 상황이 발생한다. 나도 3가 처음 해본 거라서 많이 그랬다. 근데 이게 워낙 방대한 스토리다 보니까 그냥 얘기만 듣고 하다보면 아아 예전에 이런일이 있었구나 하고 대략적인 감은 온다. 물론 세세한 스토리까진 알 수 없다.

3-5 DLC
DLC는 꼭 필요하다고 생각한다. DLC 스토리도 진짜 재밌고 DLC가 분량이 본편의 3분의2는 된다고 본다. 그리고 개인적으론 본편보다 DLC가 더 재밌었다.
