Over 50 hours were invested in playing and completing the stories of the Witcher 1 and 2 before delving into CDPR's final game in the trilogy. Another 75 hours was spent on this game alone. Although 5 days worth of time was spent playing this trilogy, The Witcher 3 (With DLC) proved it to be time well-spent. Before entering my list of pros and cons that will inevitably piss someone off because of some omission, I must preface it by saying that The Witcher 3 is probably the best game I have played with all aspects considered. Some of this game's highest highs are as follows:

Pros:
-The main characters that occupy this world truly feel alive. Our protagonists act like people who have meaningful and realistic goals. I could write a review on this one point itself, but to paraphrase: Characters like Geralt, Ciri, Triss, and Yennefer interact in dynamic relationship webs that make the world feel so alive. In games gone by, characters that Geralt knew largely interacted with just him, and not among each other. Playing from Geralt's perspective in The Witcher 3, each of the character interactions he witnesses give the impression that these people have a long history with one another.
-The graphics still hold up to snuff even after five years. Rejoice in the fact that you do not NEED any graphics mods whatsoever.
-The Witcher 3 handles the themes of moral ambiguity responsibly. Choosing between factions isn't like choosing the crap you took last night or today. Each faction or decision in the game provides noteworthy pros and cons that can make you really consider your impact on the world.
-The music of the Witcher 3 is generally exceptional for an RPG that is as bold and expansive as this
-The community has continued to prove that you can play The Witcher 3 with a diversity of playstyles regarding combat, dialogue, or general quest choices.
-Unlike many video games, The DLC in the Witcher 3 reliably hold up to these aforementioned pros. Too many video games fall in the pit trap of awkward DLCs that don't meld well with the base game.
-Gaunter O' Dimm continues to be a disturbing, yet captivating villain. His theme only bolsters his excellence as a villain.
-I understand that there isn't a large quantity of pros, but that is simply because each pro is meant to quickly and concisely praise the fundamental aspects of RPGs that this game nails.

Cons (Oh no, what do you mean this game isn't perfect!):
-The facial expressions in characters is rather shallow, While the bottom half of their faces are quite expressive, there is little movement in the eyes and brows. The characters seem to play their best poker faces above the mouth.
-While there is a lot of combat styles to choose from initially, The Witcher 3 is quite punishing for playing as sort of jack of all trades among alchemy, signs, and swordplay (which is pretty counter-intuitive to who a great Witcher should be)
-This aforementioned con effectively renders The Witcher 3's combat as a spamfest of your choosing
-While there is much enjoyment out of replaying the story, the long stretches of exploration and combat makes replaying feel somewhat tedious
-Eredin looks edgy AF under the mask
-The modding community continues to be as anemic as the rest of the trilogy
-In what I can only call an attempt to distance itself from its previous installments, The Witcher 3 unnecessarily excludes characters like Iorveth, Saskia and Siegfried to its own detriment.

Despite some technical issues that hold this game back from genuine perfection, it is a damn near replication. I truly do insist that you at least play the first two games. Otherwise, you'll look at people like Radovid or Phillapa Eilhart and say "Who the fuck is that?" Even book characters like Fringilla Vigo and Dykstra will leave non book readers confused. Despite these intimidating facts, this game is still more than enjoyable without prior knowledge. If this game is on sale, I implore you to pick it up.

게임에 대한 눈을 높여준 게임.

 위쳐3의 엔딩을 본 이후로 다른 게임을 오래하지 못하겠다. 깊은 세계관, 방대한 맵과 자유도, 다양하고 짜임새 있는 퀘스트들, 탐험지역과 각종 보상들, 개성있는 지역들과 파밍 요소까지.

 다만 게임의 템포가 조금 느리다고 생각되는 때도 있었으나 천천히 여행한다는 느낌으로 하니 상관없었다. 훌륭한 그래픽과 사운드가 있는 게임이기에 영화의 한 장면을 본다고 생각하고 즐기니 답답함이 사라졌다.
 내가 이야기를 써나간다고 생각하면 더욱 몰입감있게 플레이하게 된다. 원래 오버워치같은 빠른 템포의 게임이나 아케이드성 게임만을 거의 좋아했는데, 위쳐3를 접하고 게임에 대한 취향도 조금 바뀐 것 같다.

 장래에 게임개발자를 희망하고 있어서 명작이라길래 꾸역꾸역 시작해본 게임이지만 정말 재밌게 플레이했다. 카드게임을 좋아하지 않던 내가 궨트도 처음에는 신경도 안쓰다가 엔딩즈음에는 카드 모으면서 하나씩 도장깨기를 해나갔다.

 명작이라고 부르는 음악이나 영화도 모두에게 감동을 주지는 못하듯이 사람들이 명작, 갓겜이라고 부르는 위쳐3가 모두에게 재미있는 게임이 되지 않을 수도 있다.
 하지만 위쳐3는 정말 많은 측면에서 훌륭하게 만들어진 명작게임이라고 생각하며, 모두가 아니더라도 분명 많은 사람들에게 재미를 선사하리라고 생각한다.

 인생게임으로 포탈시리즈를 생각해왔는데, 이제는 위쳐3가 될 것 같다.





***추신***
 나는 위쳐시리즈 중에서 위쳐3만을 플레이했는데 미리 위쳐1,2 스토리라인을 알고가야만 더 재미있게 플레이할 수 있는 것 같다.

 만약에 1,2 스토리나 세계관을 찾아보지 않고서 플레이했다면 게임에 재미를 느끼지 못했을 것 같다.
 짜임새 있고 방대한 세계관과 스토리로 극찬을 받는 게임인만큼 스토리와 세계관을 어느정도 찾아보고 시작하는 것을 추천한다.
 그런 의미에서 스토리를 별로 신경쓰지 않는 사람들 혹은 스토리를 찾아보기 싫은 사람들에게는 비추천한다. 스토리 중심으로 풀려가는 게임에서 몰입감과 재미가 굉장히 떨어지리라 생각하기 때문

This is an action role-playing game that won 'A Game of the Year edition 2016'. It was designed in a massive open-world with the backdrop of magic, monsters, mysteries, and the medieval atmosphere (13th Century) based on the fictional story of The Witcher series written by Andrzej Sapkowski, a famous Polish fantasy writer. It is a sequel third-person perspective to The Witcher 2: Assassins of Kings and The Witcher. Players will take on the role of Geralt of Rivier or Butcher of Blaviken who was a legendary witcher of the School of the Wolf. His main mission in this episode is the search for his adopted daughter, Cirilla Fiona Elen Riannon (Ciri) along with tons of deeply designed gameplay, mini-game, and quest systems. Moreover, there are two outstanding expansions that were also released including Hearts of Stone and Blood and Wine. 

[b] Pros: [/b]
- Careful gameplay features and useful elements for players
- Expressive main characters and memorable supporting NPCs
- Flexibility and freedom to navigate the massive beautiful world
- Realistic and tactical combat styles for both melee or ranged weapons and a rudimentary form of magic (Sign)
- Recognizable sub-components such as chat dialogue (voice and narration), items, prostitutes, and costumes
- Absorbing story that reflects the personal conflict and the regional politics 
- Different quests and decisions that lead to distinctive storylines
- Nice mini-game design, especially Gwent the Witcher card game
- Availability of mods and other third-party applications that discriminates the gameplay style

[b] Cons: [/b]
- Long and redundant loading screen

one of the last master pieaces to ever be made

Out of the few RPG's I have played, this one seems somehow both bloated and dense with absurd amounts of loot-locations. Most dialogue choices are along the lines of 1."continue the conversation" 2."what's a (insert magical creature here)". Combat can be fun even though most fights went the same way, casting Quen and mashing the X button. Oh, I had to connect a controller once I finished the tutorial. After 50 hours I changed the difficulty to easy just to get to the end as fast as possible. I also turned off the music when it's charm began to wear off. There is an absurd amount of loot and gear to collect and sell only to slightly increase some numbers. This story has so much side content and filler that I would forget the main plot, or stop caring and skip half the dialogue. It was a little funny that petty street thugs think they were tougher than Geralt, A Witcher, A man who has seen, fought, slain, and survived things no human ever has. This game does look picturesque, but I for one believe if I'm enjoying the scenery more than anything else in an RPG then there is something wrong. Navigating this world is just a chore and the terrain usually was working against me. I had chat with someone who is a fan and he implore that I romance Triss, but the path towards that didn't look very romantic exactly. I did "Romance" Yennefer which just abruptly cut from a scene of us in a deadly gas trap to us in the bedroom... and a glitch made the sounds and visuals of her magic powers loop throughout the scene. Not too many annoying bugs that I recall other than the final boss being frozen in place for the first phase of the fight. Oh, I forgot his name due to him being so much of a non-factor in the plot for so long and when it came time to fight the Wild Hunt I just didn't feel intimidated by this devastating force (I changed the difficulty back to normal). The Hearts of Stone was very nice...I'm not sure if that was the name of the one I played but I'm definitely not playing the other DLC anytime soon. this was almost 300 hours I could have spent elsewhere. And Gwent might be the least fun minigame I have played. Most times you have to sacrifice that first round, which means you need to have weak cards in your deck to throw away. Any semblance of strategy can fall apart immediately if you don't draw the cards you need or the opponent decides to just render an entire field of units useless.
Every buck worth.

So far even though i still have a lot to go in the story i can still tell you this game is 100% worth it. The combat is easy to learn but hard to master but if combat and swords are not your thing you can always use signs as a simple way to spice up gameplay. Exploring in this game is not like others as it is actually rewarding as you can find a lot of loot that can boost you very far in the early game, the only thing to watch out for is that level scaling is disabled by start so you'll run into a lot of overleveled monsters but in my opinion this is good as you can visually see your character getting stronger and more and more op. This game definitely has its rep for a good reason and i see no excuse for not picking this game up and giving it a try.

Welcome to Honex, a division of Honesco and a part of the Hexagon Group. This is it! Wow. Wow. We know that you, as a bee, have worked your whole life to get to the point where you can work for your whole life. Honey begins when our valiant Pollen Jocks bring the nectar to the hive. Our top-secret formula is automatically color-corrected, scent-adjusted and bubble-contoured into this soothing sweet syrup with its distinctive golden glow you know as... Wait a second. Oheck it out. - Hey, those are Pollen Jocks! - Wow. I've never seen them this close. They know what it's like outside the hive. 심심하면 모션버그, 마우스버그가 일어나고 최강 괴물 사냥꾼이라면서 동네경비병한테 얻어터지는 설정오류에, 여관에서 뛰어내렸다고 낙사하는 불합리한 판정까지..

Probably the most amount of time time I've spent in a single player RPG. Combined with the GoG version, I have over 300 (specifically 215 hours on Steam, and 100 on GoG) hours spent in the game. I would 100% recommend this game to any person; the amount of detail put in this game shows how much time and passion the developers put into it. This can be seen from the world's detailed characters, the story, the subtle differences in the story dependent on the choices you make, and the detailed side quests. 5 years later, closing in on 6, I believe other developers working on open-world RPGs should strive to add the kind of detail like the Wild Hunt had (because honestly, I'm fed up with open-world games).

The only major complaint I had is with some morally ambiguous side quests, which significantly limit the player's choice, especially when considering a Witcher's arsenal. That being said, once again I'd like to emphasize, I would ABSOLUTELY recommend this game to anyone interested in a single player RPG.

하지만 그래도 고티받은 이유가 있겠지. . . . 스스로를 위안하며 게임을 다운로드하였다.


그리고 플레이 1시간, 2시간. . . 3시간이 되었을때, 나는 게임 산것을 후회하고 환불을 신청했다.

'하 , , 그래도 고티게임인데 뭔가 재밌는 매력이 있지 않을까..'


나는 뭔가에 홀리듯 위쳐3 환불신청을 취소하였고 다시 게임을 플레이하였다.


그리고 지금 이제서야 게임플레이 50시간. . . 내 감상을 요약하자면 이렇다.

I have a love-hate relationship with this game. The storyline is great. The graphics and cut scenes are well polished. The voice acting is superb. I really can't say enough good stuff about it. 

However, I don't like the grim-dark nature of the game. I don't like that the main character is a mercenary rather than a hero. I don't like that the people in it with only a few rare exceptions are so utterly wretched that you frequently get backstabbed by those that hire you or that you end up killing them because they are such horrible monsters. 

Despite all the exceptional work that went into this game, the jaded world made me go back to playing Skyrim. 

Do I recommend it for all the exceptional quality of the game, absolutely. This game isn't a product. It is art. However, it is jaded art, so I can only look at it for so long before I have to go to something else that makes me happy.

第一人称射击，美术风格跟漫画式的过场让人眼前一亮，旁白看着也挺有意思，但也就仅此而已，实际玩起来并想象中的好玩。游戏中玩家根据给出的任务目标，控制好燃料与食物资源并移动到任务目标所在飞船上开始搜索，同时获取其他的材料资源来打造武器等装备，也就是捡垃圾的部分了。

之所以说想象中的好玩，主要是FPS方面实在一点都不爽，射击手感也不太好，单纯是捡垃圾的话很容易生腻。当然中途觉得腻了，你也可以选择当跑酷党，一上来就去舰桥下载地图，然后直接冲着目标零件去就行，如果选择全搜的话就太浪费时间了，仅适合热衷于捡垃圾的玩家。

游戏品质是没有太大问题，不过要说最影响游戏体验的话，还是路痴DEBUFF，真的是恶心人。说是有25%机率移动到错误的地方，但是每次都是等到我移动到有任务目标的飞船上时触发，还不是一两次而是连着四五次，本身食物跟燃料都是足够的，这样一搞下来就全都消耗完了，只能被迫登陆到飞船上捡垃圾。

如果制作组是想通过这种恶心人的DEBUFF来强行增加玩家的游玩时间，那么只会适得其反，游戏比较独特的美术风格也难以挽救过于重复的场景设计，本身FPS打得也不是那么爽，捡垃圾捡着捡着特别烦躁，加上音乐听多了也很烦，玩到中途就直接开着外置音乐来降低烦躁感了。你再搞个这么恶心人的DEBUFF，要是机率真像数字标着倒还好，遇到了只能说脸黑，连着五次都这样我只想对着制作人一拳超人。

另外提醒一下感兴趣且准备入手的玩家，中途打了一定进度的时候千万不要再在主菜单点新游戏，特喵直接是把整个进程给重置了，还以为是换掉当轮游玩的人物重新游玩，所以千万别手贱XD可惜的是游戏并不支持多人，要是多人联机捡垃圾打打怪，游戏重复性过高的问题估计也不会被那么多玩家吐槽了。

打完血与酒，点开巫师三十周年庆视频，不知道是不是自己太过矫情，看了三遍哭了三遍，从希里推进白葡萄园大门和雷吉斯拥抱就有点没忍住（刚做完血与酒感觉真的很对不起雷吉斯，因为我的选择让他陷入了本不应该陷入的麻烦），后面一个个熟悉面孔都出来后就绷不住了，好希望这就是我的结局，经历了那么多，还能和老友在一起在庄园里打昆特牌，还能在某个不起眼的夜晚偷偷摸到安娜葡萄园偷葡萄吃，能在酩酊大醉后每天早上从自己的床上起来而不是某个荒原冥想起来，床边上还是那个一袭黑衣，紫罗兰色的眼睛，发色漆黑如渡鸦，丁香和醋栗香味的女人，瞥我一眼，什么都不说

一路上做的选择太多，事后大多后悔，走的路太远，故事无不荒凉。从我割下大狮鹫的头，赶着夕阳余晖策马穿过百草园踏上寻女征程开始，我留下太多足迹。战乱横飞，怪物肆虐的威伦，浸泡在轰雷下雨不断阴郁的空气里，发酵着绝望，痛苦和艰难。盖坦在这里因为绝望屠村，血腥男爵在这里寻求救赎，我也是在这杀过逃兵，却也捡到他的家人寄给他问其归期的信件，里面有他女儿天真热切的语言。然后我到了诺诚，卷入过政治斗争，因为特莉斯血洗教徒护卫。乘船跌到史凯利杰群岛，因为这里慕强之风受到了少有的尊敬，也回到凯尔莫罕，我的老家，在这里最后一役击退了狂猎，同时也牺牲了如父亲般对待我老狼派的维瑟米尔，要是你在白酒园肯定会更加热闹吧。我也在迷雾之岛找回了我的女儿，一路上奔波的劳顿迷茫之心终于得到了解脱，因为只要找到她一切都是值得的。大战后我救了欧吉尔德，只是那个他已不爱，只记得要爱的爱丽丝，永远地离开了这个悲剧的世界。
我最终留在了陶森特，这个温柔如糖果般的阳光下有着美酒，葡萄的暖色调公国。多年后在葡萄园重读探险收集的信件，难免想起那一个个不大不小的选择，改变了他们的人生，也塑造了现在的杰洛特。那个不轻易评判他人，未知全貌谨慎行事，也明白自身渺小的杰洛特。

Simultaneously the best single-player RPG story I've experienced to date and most infuriating movement controls and mechanics.  A wonderful "Winds howling."/10.

- "Run Roach!" *Gets stuck on literally nothing while passing through an archway or over a small bridge.*
- *Swims in awkward circles trying to get the loot option to pop up at the bottom of a lake or bay.*
- *Gets stuck on something while trying to swim out of a cave, sunken ship, or underwater ruin with 30% breath left.*
- *Ignites candles, chandeliers, & lanterns while trying to loot a crate or chest.*
- *While fighting multiple enemies at once.* "Damnit Geralt, quit targeting that one!"
- *While attempting to run from an enemy because you're so fed up fighting it while passing through that area.* "QUIT TURNING AROUND!"
- *Whistles to call Roach.* *Meanwhile in a non-parallel universe Roach spawns a mile away and gets stuck on every fence, tree, bush, and BLADE OF GRASS on his way over.*
- *Fight starts and Geralt decides to use his fists.*  "Geralt, draw your sword.  Geralt, draw your sword please?  Geralt, draw your @!#$!%& sword!"
- *Geralt trips while going down stairs.*
- *Geralt decides it's time to ski back down an incline.*
- *After a cutscene Geralt's decoctions disappear, is thrown into a fight, and those same decoctions haven't been replenished.*

In all seriousness, this game was quite the adventure.  I got the worst ending not just once, but twice because at the time there were no in-depth guides on how to get the best ending.  I also failed several side quests I wasn't aware needed to be completed before progressing the main story or that were dependent upon certain outcomes.  In each of those playthroughs I had at least 100%'d White Orchard and Skellige or Velen/Novigrad as well.  It hurt - was demoralizing even.  After a few years of putting it off I'd finally built up the willpower to complete the perfect playthrough - the best Witcher 2 simulation, the best endings to all quests and story lines, all quests completed (none failed), and 100%'d every area (White Orchard, Velen/Novigrad, Skellige, & Toussaint).  I've done it.  It is finished and I can finally head into New Game+ knowing that I at least did everything right or to my liking at least once.

A few things I would have changed about this perfect playthrough:
- Set the difficulty to Death March before starting and kept it there for the achievement. (My perfect playthrough being my 3rd or 4th time, I didn't want to deal with the struggle.  In hindsight, I think I could have handled it.)
- Disabled enemy upscaling. (This is because there were level 1 rats during a quest which would instakill Geralt due to a problem in the enemy upscaling option.  You can also tell when some enemies fail to get upscaled, some don't, and others receive a buff.  Disabling this option would have also made Death March easier.)

Folks have plenty of gripes with the game and it does need a few quality of life changes but we'll probably never see another update as they're working on a new Witcher game that doesn't feature Geralt as the main protagonist.  I personally was annoyed by some of the bugs I encountered in Toussaint.  It shows that they either pushed the expansion out before it was ready or the development skills of members who might've helped with Cyberbug 2077 started showing:
- White horses spawning in the middle of the street stuck inside each other or blocking doorways into shops.
- NPCs' limbs and clothes going invisible.
- After leaving the merchant near the Nilfgardian embassy the world disintegrated.  Walls, doors, many things went invisible and couldn't be interacted with.  I could see under the map and had to load an old save just to resolve the issue.

Regardless, this is definitely a game - no, a world and a life - worth experiencing.

Tips for new players:
- Take your time.  Take in the sights, smell the flowers, savor the conversations, relationships, and story.  No reason to feel rushed or push until after your first playthrough.
- Save your game often and as a new save!  Quicksave is F5 on the keyboard otherwise you'll need to enter the menu and manually save if using a controller.  You'll never know when something bad will happen (death, outcomes, etc.) and will need/want to reload to try again.
- Play Gwent at every opportunity.  Win cards from opponents and diffuse bad situations while you still can.
- Take all posts from notice boards.  They consist of contracts, side quests, and points of interest.
- Do side quests, contracts, treasure hunts, and explore points of interest before progressing the story.
- Purchase maps from vendors if available - especially in Skellige - to reveal fast travel signs.
- Read your Bestiary for tips on what oils, signs, and potions to use against monsters.
- Loot monsters.
- Loot destroyed monster nests.
- Loot everything out of a guards sight.
- Sell common, magic, and master-crafted weapons and armor you don't need.  Keep relics.
- Dismantle junk for materials or sell it.
- Craft oils, potions, and decoctions as you can.
- Only craft Witcher gear (Cat, Griffin, Wolf, Viper, Manticore, &/or Bear).  There are very few relics that end up actually being better than these sets.  I personally went with Cat (light, bleeds, faster attacks) and upgraded it to Grandmaster.  You can get all the diagrams for every set however it's more efficient to choose and stick with one.
- After killing a monster that grants a trophy, ensure Roach still has the trophy you want equipped.

Great game-play, complex stories, fleshed out characters. Plus Gwent gets addicting!

The Witcher. Loved this game was something new and pretty different from all the RPG's I have played in the past.
Assassin of Kings. This actually disappointed me a lot. I just couldn't engage with the story. The visual looked and feel felt totally over saturated like an 80's American TV show. I finished it but I really just ran through the campaign played the Roach path and nothing more.
Wild Hunt. Trumps the lot. This game is a master piece and might just be my favourite ever RPG. At first I found the visual style again a little jarring. The light and dark of sunshine and clouds seemed a little too abrupt for me. However I soon got over that once the story kicked into gear. The Characters are great and even the filler is entertaining. Just playing Gwent and being a jobbing Witcher is a lot of fun. Now 99 hours in and nearing the end of the Blood and Wine expansion I can say that everybody should play this game. Other developers should take note of the great story and characters. I have never read any of the books but I assume we all have Andrzej Sapkowski to thank for giving CD Project Red these great characters to work with.

Let's hope this is not the last game we see from them in the Witcherverse as it would be really nice to follow Ciri on some adventures. She is definitely my highlight of the game.

Took me 5 years to beat this game because I restarted 3 times after taking long breaks from the game and forgetting the story. I actually have 2 copies of this game, one for PS4 and another one for the PC when I upgraded. With the Pandemic and losing internet connection several times, I decided to gave this game another shot. The story of this game is amazing if you stick with it in the beginning. It's kind of a slow burner in the beginning but once you get deep in the story, it gets really entertaining. The characters in this game are memorable and the voice acting of the main characters are amazing. I'm more of a First Person Shooter and Real Time Strategy guy but this game is the exception when it comes to single players and the only single player game that I have beaten in the last 5 years. The last single player game I beat was Mass Effect 3. Unlike Cyberpunk, this game is 5 years old so the bugs are rare, the DLC is cheap, graphics are still good, and this game has aged pretty well. Actually, the DLC's for this game puts other full games to shame. Only con I have is that the combat can be better and Roach (the horse) keeps getting stuck in terrain, minor annoyances. Other then that, this game is great.

Of course this game is a complete masterpiece, what else needs to be said about it that hasn't already been said? I have over 200 hours on it on GOG and recently purchased it on Steam to have everything in the same place. 

In short - combat is amazing, visceral, fluid and responsive. Geralt does fast attack, slow ones, dodges, parries, leaps to the side and rolls just like you've come to expect from your action games. There's an insane amount of combinations you can do with your attacks, alchemy concoctions, signs and general monster-hunting equipment like bombs, traps and similar. No build is useless and everything can be utilized to beat the game even on its hardest difficulty.

The story is an emotional and gripping one and well worth all those hours you have to put in it to finish it. It has various different endings, some of which are diametrically opposite to each other. DLCs are just as good as the main story, with Blood and Wine being slightly better in my opinion.

I won't go any further, it's unecessary as the game already won numerous awards and has so many good reviews that it's almost redundant. Would be worth full 60 euros even in 2020.

Gripping story, compelling characters. If you're going into this without having played W1 and W2, I'd suggest to play them first. W1's controls are horrible, but it is worth it for the story, it gives context for all that is to come, and a LOT is to come. I couldn't wait to get to W2, which was a nice upgrade in a lot of gameplay-related areas, if not directly the story part (a bit short).

To give a bit of context, I started to play W3 in 2020 Oct. or so, not having played the previous games. Felt like I was missing something, lots of references to past events and experiences, in dialogs with characters, and it annoyed me a more and more as it went on. So about ~80 hours in, I bit the bullet, put W3 down, bought W1+W2 on GOG, and started again from the very beginning. And I gotta tell you, it was WORTH IT!

64 hours of W1, 45 hours of W2, and finally, ~200 hours after starting a new W3 game from the W2 save, it is done. And what a ride it was. Ups and downs, lovable characters, hatable characters, lovable AND hatable characters, hard decisions, and overall an emotional rollercoaster. It was never boring, not even the most menial of sidequests. And I'm sad that it came to an end, but every story has to end once.

And I've gotta say, the two DLC-s are a must, (but buy them in a discounted GOTY package or so). The DLC-s help with the letting to part, and help is needed after such an epic arc of a story. They are both good little stories of their own, with a kind of an extended conclusion to the main story (BnW even takes place in a beatiful, whole new fairytale-like area).

If you like story-rich adventure games, and a bit of hack-n-slash (this is more the former), liked the series, then you'll most probably enjoy it. and I'd suggest you to give it a go!

Without a doubt the most boring trite I have ever played in my life. On a technical level it's a good game, it runs well, it's pretty and there is clearly a lot of love put into the game. However, the game itself is absolutely mind-numbing. 

The gameplay is very similar to the 'Batman: Arkham' games with combos and gadgets, but unlike the Arkham games the Witcher 3's combat is not only easy and hilariously abusable - its also boring. The game gives you the tools to make combat irrelevant in the form of signs, oils and decoctions: Igni does a ludicrous amount of damage, Quen makes you functionally invincible if you have a brain, decoctions buff you to absurd degrees and  oils skyrocket your damage even further. Raising the difficulty makes no appreciable difference, enemies simply have more health and damage which just forces you to rely more on the trivialising signs and oils. Bosses are similarly meaningless; just bigger enemies to annihilate with said tools. The only time the game is difficult is when enemies swarm you and lunge at you from off-screen to one-shot you but it doesn't matter because I have quen and I'm fucking invincible. 

"Just don't use signs or oils if they make the game so easy," I hear you say from your highchair. Let's just ignore that doing so removes half the game and go with it, and well now suddenly you just added 50 extra hours to the gameplay because enemies now have one million health. If you have even the slightest amount of skill in any video game with a functioning combat system you wont need oils and are probably saving time by not wandering around for hours picking up plants. None of this would be a problem if it wasn't all so fucking boring, the combat can be easy if its fun but it isn't. It's clunky, it's slow. It is boring.


Maybe the puzzles are really good? Sadly, there are no puzzles in Witcher 3. Despite what you may think, walking into a room and turning on [strike]detective-vision[/strike] Witcher-sense and hunting for pixels is not a puzzle, you are not solving anything by following a bread crumb trail, you are not solving anything by listening to Geralt tell you what to do and you are not solving anything by hunting glowing pixels until you are to allowed to leave. "Puzzles" in Witcher are time-wasters to make people think that they are actually solving shit when really you're just walking around in circles, listening to boring dialogue and clicking on glowing buttons like a conditioned ape. Now that I think about it... puzzles in Witcher are very similar to the puzzles in Dora the Explorer.


Well it is a story based game, so surely the story is good. To be honest I never finished the story, I had better things to do with my time run than between 50 npcs to have boring conversations about the lives of paper-thin, predictable carcitures. As to how far i got in the story itself, I stopped playing a few hours after the 'Bloody Baron' questline. Lets go over the 'Bloody Baron' questline. (Spoiler warning) It is a story that covers the tragedy of a poor baron who beats his wife and child to within an inch of their lives and now feels sad that they left him :'(. Your role in this is that he knows your estranged god-like super-soldier adopted daughter that you, for whatever reason, are worried about and he has knowledge of her whereabouts so now are forced to do several hours of soul crushingly boring I-Spy puzzles and meaningless dialogue with a wife beater you're a supposed to feel sorry for. People often point to this part of the game as being an excellent story with great characters which may be true if you have never engaged in any media beyond spongebob. However this is supposed to be an adult story for adult brains which is funny considering the gameplay until this point treats you as if you are 6 years old. It's kinda concerning, especially when you consider that most of the target audience for this game aren't going to be able to pick up on any of the themes it almost conveys or take it in the completely wrong direction since gamers arent known for their respect for women. Thankfully the quest ends and you are given your precious information about how your princess is in another castle which from what i am told is basically how the rest of the game goes. I was not able to get much deeper information about the rest of the story as anyone else I know fails to remember anything beyond brief moments and/or the dreams they had falling asleep while playing. If you're crying from your crib while reading my review, answer this; do YOU remember the story? 


What it all really comes down to is that Witcher 3 is a babys-first RPG masquerading as something more. Its combat is clunky, unfun and abusable, its story is shallow and predictable, the RPG elements are limited to being Geralt and worst of all, in a medium with one major goal, The Witcher 3 is boring. Despite all that I have said, I understand that enjoyment is subjective and you're allowed to enjoy The Witcher 3. Jesus, it can even be your favourite game for all I care. However, quality is objective and Witcher 3 is objectively a bad game and the people who treat it like some incredible masterpiece that changed the landscape of video games are just wrong. When Dark Souls came out it was so beloved that it inspired an entire genre known as 'Souls-likes' some of which went on to great success such as: Star-Wars Fallen Order and Nioh. Even other nerd games like Hollow Knight are heavily inspired by Dark Souls. I struggle to find refrences or any game made in The Witchers image, 6 years since this game came out, and yet no one bothers to make a copycat game or anything like it. Let me repeat - it is not because the game is too perfect to imitate, but because anyone with understanding of video games knows that not only is it a shit game and isn't worth replicating, but no game developer wants to be in crunch time for two years to make endless mediocre quests with the same writing level as Skyrim. 


If you're looking for an RPG experience that isnt a complete waste of time I suggest the following:

Misc: Horizon Zero Dawn, Red Dead Redemption 2, Final Fantasy 7 (I hear the remake is good), Vampyre the masquerade bloodlines (combat is crap but the story is vast), Bioshock 1 (not much of RPG but one of the best stories in video gaming), Undertale (despite the memes it is legitimately a good game with a lot of heart)

Sci-fi: Fallout New Vegas, Mass Effect 1-3,  Deus Ex Human Revolution, Deus Ex Mankind Divided, the original Deus Ex (2000) is a legitimate masterpiece but it's like 20 years old and I wouldn't recommend it unless you are prepared for something so outdated

Fantasy: Dragon Age, Skyrim (much like Witcher it's an extremely entry level RPG but even though it's shit it's at least fun), Monster Hunter World (stories on the low end but combat-wise it's a lot of fun) DARK SOULS 1-3 (I am biased in this as it is my favourite game but it does something Witcher 3 never does... let go of your hand)

Isn't this the next level Oblivion crap? The reason I bought this game was because I had heard so much about the story and its narration, which after 24 hours of playing I still don't find it that appealing. I have forced myself so many times to come back to this game and finish it just for the sake of giving it the benefit of the doubt and boy every time it disappoints so much.
It's overloaded with weird bugs and glitches that Oblivion looks like a child next to it. So many times I've been fallen into weird unrecoverable glitches that now I feel scared to explore the world and do the side quests.
The gameplay is painful, the graphics and story are overrated (even for the time of its release), the controls are awkward. It cannot decide if it's a strategy game or role playing. There is too much unnecessary details and items and crap in it that just makes it overly complicated. What difference does it really make if you ate cheese or candy? Is this the hallmark of the required "thinking" that the game wants me to do? Why is there so much crap?
If you are looking for a game that is so bad it makes you laugh then this is it. The dialogues and voice overs are badly done. The AI is awkward and hilarious. It's Oblivion all over again but not even funnier.
The soundtrack is this constant drone like noise made by badly played hurdy-gurdy, it could give the instrument a bad reputation.

[h1]Сюжет[/h1]
Сюжет в игре добротный, но не сказать, что один из найлучших. Действия начнутся в Белом Саду и по ходу развития сюжета ведьмак побывает в Новиграде, Велене, на Скеллиге и даже в Каэр Морхене(куда без этого). Присутствует вариативность и несколько концовок, которые зависят от твоего выбора в прошлых актах(у меня вышла самая лучшая концовка :) ). За время прохождения ты начинаешь сопереживать персонажам и каждая смерть может даться нелегко...
Насчет сайд квестов. Я бы их разделил на неплохие и хорошие. Неплохие заключаются в помощи обычным людям, в основном это поиск и убийство чудовища(или чудовищ). Хорошие - это помощь ярлам или второстепенным персонажам (тем же чародейкам). Такие квесты могут быть как рофельные так и довольно страшные(добавляют нотку хоррора). Эти 2 группы сайд квестов могут сливаться, что означает, что могут быть как интересные квесты от простых горожан, так и обычные поиски и убийства существ для более значимых особей.
[h1]Графика[/h1]
Графика на то время хорошая, глаза не мылит. Честно говоря, выйди игра на несколько лет позже и мой комп (у меня ryzen 2400g со встроенной графикой) не потянул бы эту великую игру. На минималках игра идёт в 30 фпс и что удивительно, игра идёт плавно и не чувствуются как 30. Оптимизация на уровне.
[h1]Геймплей[/h1]
Играем мы за Геральта и иногда за Цириллу. Как основное оружие у белого волка идёт стальной меч против людей и серебряный против чудовищ, дополняется всё это арбалетом и знаками. Временно усилить персонажа можно с помощью масел, зелий и бомб. Следует напомнить, что это РПГ игра и тут есть уровни и система скиллов. За каждый уровень даётся 1 поинт, с помощью которого ты можешь улучшить характеристики. НО так же поинты можно получить у местах силы.
Честно сказать, удивительно, что геймплей, которым заключается на убийство врагов одним мечем, может не надоедать и даже наоборот - завлекать.
Так же присутствуют увороты и блоки, что, как по мне, сильно улучшает геймплей.
[h1]Музыкальное сопровождение[/h1]
Музыка передаёт всю необходимую атмосферу, но меня не зацепила. Она не надоедает, но и не запоминается. Меньше всего мне понравился ОСТ на Скеллиге.
[h1]Бестиарий[/h1]
В игре присутствует огромное количество разных чудовищ со своим ЛОРом. Чудовища поделяются на 10 разных видов, у которых есть свои слабые и сильные стороны. В зависимости от вида монстра к нему нужно применять разный подход(если играешь на низких сложностях, то можно и не париться насчёт этого, хотяя...).  Так же есть отдельные существа: Дикая Охота, которая не входит в бестиарий(хотя гончие входят).
[h1]Послесловие[/h1]
Для меня The Witcher 3 - это лучшая сингл РПГ игра, на которую нужно ровняться остальным играм этого жанра. В игре есть и мелкие баги, которые не влияют на общее впечатление. Больше всего мне не нравится "дефекты" с речью персонажей. ПОСТОЯННО в диалоге речь всех персонажей, кроме гг, то немного замедлена, то немного ускорена. А может это у меня дефекты...
Игра стоит свою цену, возможно должна стоить даже больше. Ставлю 9/10 "зараза".

Quick disclaimer. I have more bad to say about this game than good, buuuuut despite all the criticism it is a great game and a must buy IF it is on sale. DO NOT pay full price for this. The idea that in 2015 people paid 60 dollars for this is truly upsetting. 

Pros
- Interesting world based on the Witcher Novels with lots to explore.
- Tons of side quests that are actually more fun than the main quest.
- Main characters have a ton of personality. 
- Side Characters are super interesting and make you want to learn more about their lives and the world of Witcher.
- The soundtrack is cool, and the music in the open world is great! Walking by bards is a treat!

Cons
- Playing the game suuuuuucks. Fighting monsters literally felt like a chore and a distraction from story. 
- The controls are super clunky. Imagine that someone poured a bunch of syrup on your joystick. 
- Combat is repetitive and hard due to the bad controls. Easy difficulty is preferable, as combat is not the games forte.
- The loot button is the same button as sprint, so you will often overshoot the thing you are trying to loot. 
- There is too much loot, and you will constantly get new gear that is slightly better than the old stuff. 
- On top of there being too much gear, all of that gear has durability and is very annoying to manage. 
- You have a carry capacity, and always too much loot to hold, so you are constantly overburdened. 
- There is a complex crafting system! That you will literally never need to use since the game will give you good gear.
- You can only fast travel from a fast travel point. Meaning if you are in the middle of nowhere you have to walk to one!
- The game is really starting to show it's age. 
- Cutscenes in this game look worse than the open world, and they pop up constantly. 
- Voice acting volume is constantly changing between cutscenes, open world, and loading screens. 
- Tons of bugs, some that stop you from being able to do quests. 
- The main story is a bit dull after you meet up with Ciri. Finding her is the good part of the game. 

Now I know what you are thinking, why should I get this game if there are so many problems with it? It's because the world and it's inhabitants are so interesting and fun to interact with, that it negates all of the problems with the game. Especially when the game is on sale, it's honestly a steal to get this game for 10 bucks. The game is at it's best when you view it as an interactive story book, where you have to swing your sword around sometimes. If this game didn't have a super easy mode, I don't think I could suggest it. 

 I will say though there are a few mods that are a must get if you buy this game. Both of them are on NexusMods for free and I'll link them below. The first lets you fast travel whenever you want, the second gets rid of carry capacity, and the third gets rid of weapon durability. 

1. Fast Travel: https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/324
2. Carry Capacity:  https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/342
3. Weapon Durability: https://www.nexusmods.com/witcher3/mods/3

They certainly dumbed down the series since the last release. Sure it raked in a larger audience but it sacrificed what is was previously in the process, which was once well written and unique instead became copy-paste and bland. The game completely omits that Triss did nothing but screw Geralt over with everything she's done involving for the Sorceresses Lodge, most likely just to have her as a romance option. This isn't the only thing it omits either.

Nothing says player hand holding like Geralt's *detective vision* removing any player involvement in questing, and Geralt's permanent monologuing to ensure the player knows what they're seeing scene to scene on the rollercoaster. Most quests will follow the same formula of: Talk to person > get told to go somewhere > while at somewhere, learn the person you talked to first is morally dubious > but so is the new person you meet in the quest > get money from either person and move on to next quest.

The combat isn't as good as the predecessor, there's very little thought put into it, which isn't really Witcher-like. Unsure how many variants of "x time of day" wraith there really are, but they're overused in this. 

Gwent was a good minigame, and the minigame shouldn't be more enjoyable than the actual game. Unfortunately, Gwent was also poorly balanced. Fixed by the spin off expansion of Gwent that you can play instead with more balance, but more child friendly too, because swearing is bad.

One of the greatest action RPG's I've ever played

If there's one complaint I'd [i] have [/i] to choose, it'd be that the expansiveness of the quests and it's overwhelming map size outpaces the variety of The Witcher 3's combat system, which proves to be a bit too simple after spending hundreds of hours hacking and slashing

But despite weaknesses in Geralt's agility and fighting styles, the game has consistently provided me with comfort and immersion with it's dense narrative, fantastic script writing, and an endlessly enchanting adaptation of it's wildly interesting base material

While having nearly 800 hours in a single player game may imply that I have done multiple playthroughs, that is not the case here, I have one save file on PS4 (276 hours) and one save file on PC (500+ hours, haven't finished Blood and Wine on PC)... that's really all that you should need to tell you about how much I really like this game. That being said, it's time demanding approach to the open world genre will either hit or miss depending on how much you are willing to dedicate to one game, so definitely keep that in mind before purchasing

1) 전투 부분

죽음의 행군 난이도로 플레이. 레벨 스케일링 중간에 킴. 검술위쳐로 플레이.

초반 전투는 길가 몹에게도 툭하면 죽을 정도로 난이도는 상당히 빡세고, 똥손인 본인은 후반에도 무쌍을 찍지 못하였음.
난이도가 낮은 보스는 금방깻지만, 다른사람들도 어려워하는 보스몹은 짧게는 한시간(진), 길게는 4시간걸린 적도 있고(이리스의 두려움) , 몹끼임현상으로 간신히 깬 경우도 있음(개구리왕자).

정말 욕도하면서 깻지만 그만큼 성취감도 컷음.  멘탈이 강하고 전투센스가 있다면 무조건 죽행 난이도로 플레이 하기를 권장.
몇몇 사람들이 위쳐는 전투에서 재미를 느낄 생각을 하지 말라하지만, 본인은 당당하게 말할 수 있음. 전투에서도 자신만의 파훼법을 찾아가는 맛이있다고. 나중에 몇몇 공략들을 보았을 때, 다른 사람들이 어떻게 보스를 공략하였는지를 보았을 때, 나와의 다른점을 보는 맛도 재미있었음.

다만, 전투 중 불편함이 있을 때가 많았음(자동 목표 변경 때문에 목표로 한적을 안때리고 다른 놈을 때리는 현상).
PC판이라 그런 것일수도?? 

2) 스토리 부분

본편 초반은 상당히 지루하고 몰입하기 힘들었음. 이는 너무나 방대하고 긴 스토리를 언제 다 깨냐는 마음 때문이기도 하였음.
그래서 몇몇 부가퀘스트, 위쳐의뢰를 안하기로 마음먹은 후, 내가 하고 싶은 것만 깨니 상당히 몰입도 잘되고 스페이스바를 연타하는 일도 줄어들었음. 

스토리가 재미있어지는 부분은 레벨 15 정도 부터 였음. 본편 엔딩을 본 후의 허탈함은 은근 여운을 남겨주었음.

특히 DLC Hearts of stone은 가장 몰입도가 높았고 여운도 컸고 재미있었음.
blood and wine은 그것보단 약간 덜했지만, 마지막 보스의 위용이 절망적으로 보여서 인상을 남김(그러나 쉽게 깸).

개인적으로 본편보다 확장팩을 훨씬 재미있게 플레이함. 꼭 확장팩까지 플레이하기를 권장(멋있는 아이템도 얻을 수 있기에).

온라인 게임만 해오던 나에게 싱글게임의 맛은 어떠한 것인지 제대로 알려준 게임이라고 생각함.


총 평가 : 4.7/5.0

마지막으로 첨언을 하자면, 될 수 있으면 공략은 최대한 보지 않고 플레이 하기를 권장.
왜냐하면 스포일러를 당할 수 있기 때문 ㅠ.ㅠ
본인도 최대한 안보았지만 어쩔 수 없이 당한 경우도 있어서.

Great atmosphere, tense action, very dense world, which is exciting to explore.

The Witcher 3 is as dense and deep as the other two games in the series in terms of RPG mechanics, and the overwhelmingly massive open-world environment has at once made that depth more intimidating, and in the long run, more rewarding. It’s difficult to express just how huge and open this world is: verdant, rolling fields liberally dotted with swaying foliage of every shape and size fill the space between loosely connected, ramshackle townships where people struggle to scrape by. A full day/night cycle and dynamic weather pull it all together, cementing The Witcher 3’s landscape as one of the most authentic-feeling open worlds I’ve ever seen. A handy minimap points you where you want to go, which might seem like a crutch, but honestly, without it, I’d have been hopelessly lost. That a world this size still feels so purposeful, and full of things to do is quite an achievement.
long campaign
A masterpiece!

Amazing world and very fun to play, would recommend downloading some texture&hair mods to enhance. Great fun, but if you try to 100% it, don't forget to complete all side quests before continuing the main story. did that 80h ago, now i have one failed quest :(

They added a jump button! that really annoyed me not being able to jump in the other Witcher games. What is there to say that others haven't? The game excels in the storytelling, writing and voice acting. CD Project Red make it all look easy, play other games and most seem to really struggle with these things.

To be honest, i'm not in love with this series in the way a lot of other people are, the actual gameplay never did anything for me, and i'm not that into fantasy RPG's, but the storytelling is so strong that it can rise above the mediocre gameplay.

A few things i didn't like... being able to fail quests, there's a few times where depending on your actions or lack of actions you will fail a quest, they don't warn you either, that annoyed me.

Also there was one time where a dialogue choice lead me to fight a key character to the death, and i didn't realize that's what would happen or i wouldn't have picked it! 

Anyway, you get your moneys worth, this is a huge game, the expansion Blood and Wine adds a whole new kingdom and region which adds like 50 more hours of story, it's worth checking out if you haven't played it yet.

Amazing.

One of the best games I have ever played. You will forever be comparing everything you play to the Witcher 3 and it will frustrate you, because most other games cannot compete with this beast. If you play games like Fallout, Skyrim, Red Dead, The Last of Us or Batman Arkham series - we probably have similar taste. So here are some of the reasons why the Witcher tops them all: 

1. Narrative: the main story is good, but the side quests and DLC's are fantastic. Quests require you to investigate, hunt, fight and make the right choices. Outcomes vary massively and can depend on the tiniest decision.
2. The characters and dialogue: there is a fair amount of talking in this game, but its interactive and written so well. If you like story-driven games, you won't be bored.
3. The amount of detail is staggering. The scenery, the cities, the people, the various landscapes, hidden locations - some crazy attention to detail went into this game and most people will never find it.
4. The gameplay is awesome and you can switch play-style deep into the game (i.e. move from magic to alchemy to combat at any point). Its satisfying, yet never overly easy. 
5. I can't leave a review and not mention the music. You might not notice it much at first, but the soundtrack to this game is phenomenal and it adds so much to the overall experience.

If you love fantasy, gritty fairy tales, romance, interesting stories and good humour, play it. 
If you're a gamer, play it.

왜 사람들이 위쳐3를 명작이라 하는지 알 수 있었습니다.
이 게임은 전투자체는 무난한 3D액션 RPG로 비슷한 시기에 나왔던 게임이나 이전세대에 나온 AAA급 게임들에 비해 크게 특별하지 않습니다.
그리고 수많은 버그(팅김 및 무한 로딩, 특정지점에서 세이브 불가, 글리치 등등) 덕에 수십시간을 날려먹는 경험도 겪어봤습니다. 

여기까지 본다면 그냥 똥겜이다 싶겠지만 이 게임의 스토리텔링과 컷신 연출이 위의 모든 단점을 매꾸고도 남을 정도로 유저들을 빠져들게 합니다.
저도 왠만한 지루한 대화는 그냥 스킵하곤 하지만 플레이하면서  컷신을 넘기지 않고 하나하나에 몰입하면서 보게됬습니다.

중간중간 미니게임 식으로 격투나 경마등도 있지만 궨트에 빠지니 시간가는줄 몰랐습니다.

다만 게임 스토리 볼륨이 어마어마하다보니 1회차때 모든 요소를 즐기셨다면 뉴겜+로 2회차를 즐길땐 좀 지치곤 합니다.

3줄로 요약하자면
1. 전투도 특별한거 없고 버그도 많다.
2. 위의 모든 단점을 스토리텔링과 미니게임(궨트)이 압도하고 남는다.
3. 볼륨이 너무커서 2회차할때 지친다.

My only complaint is Geralt riding his horse instead of riding me.

YES YES YES!!! This is a MUST BUY if you like open world games. Although it is a little annoying to explore freely only to find you can't go somewhere because you need an item but other than that great game!

I've poured so many hours of my life into The Witcher 3 and I still can't get enough. I've played this game on the Xbox, the Switch, and now on the PC. 

Geralt of Rivia and his grand adventures take you through, quite literally, hell and back in this game. The combat mechanics can be somewhat lacking at times and repetitive, you'll quickly learn how important it is to level up Geralt and unlock skills to ease the pain of redundancy of the ever recurring battles that he faces. At the beginning, it can be seem like a daunting task to learn about the sword techniques and the use of Signs but eventually you will get the hang of it and rely on both of them to get you through each day.

As for the graphics, the scenery in the masterfully crafted game is absolutely incredible. It's easy to stand at the top of a mountain or a castle and just look around to take in the sights. The trees, the rivers/creeks, the lakes, even the little forest critters are beautiful. The sounds that go with the imagery are just as amazing, it makes you feel like you are there with Geralt.

Another really fascinating and enjoyable part of this game is the crafting and the alchemy. I love that it's not as easy as just going to a merchant and buying whatever you need and then going back to your quest. You have to travel out to the wilderness and search for herbs or pieces of equipment to better your gear and potions. It's a really immersive game and that takes up so much time but I do find it to be a lot of fun, like a treasure hunt. 

Lastly, the story! Do not ever skip the cut scenes! The story in this game is so detailed and keeps you hooked. Another interesting part of this game is that every choice you make impacts the outcome of a lot the quests so that's partly the reason why you should pay attention to the cut scenes as well.

I love this game and I try to convince as many people as I can to play it just so they can experience it firsthand like I did all those years ago. I've played it many times and it's still my favorite game!

Phenomenal game with rich, gritty and deep stories all the way from the main quest line down to the smallest of side quests. This game puts forth an immersive realistic and morally ambiguous fantasy world in which, much like real life, you're often faced with dilemmas and the right course of action is not always apparent. You'll at times be faced with seemingly simple decisions that turn out to have far reaching consequences, trying to do the right thing can backfire dramatically and many times there is no right thing to do at all as you're forced to choose between multiple negative outcomes. If you like dark, story driven 3rd person action RPG's and you somehow haven't played this yet then I highly recommend it!
Absolutely Fantastic!

[h1]갓겜, 그 이상 말이 더 필요한지?[/h1]
왜 다들 그렇게 갓쳐 갓쳐 하는지 최다 GOTY수상을 했는지 절실하게 이해가 가는 게임이었다.

솔직히 초반에는 조작감이 적응이 덜 돼서 재미있다는 느낌은 크게 받지 못했다.

하지만 베스미어와 함께 그리핀을 잡을 때, 그리핀을 보고 '와 존내 무섭게 생겼네 날아댕기기까지 하는데 저걸 어떻게 잡아 ㄷㄷ' 하면서 미끼, 석궁, 표식 등등 온갖 수단을 동원하여 마침내 그리핀을 잡아내고 느낀 그 뿌듯함.

퀘스트 완료 표시가 뜨며 와-하는 그 효과음이 들린 순간 가슴이 웅장해지며 나는 위쳐에 급속도로 빠져들게 되었다.

그 뒤 본격적으로 플레이를 하기 위해 몇 개의 편의성 모드를 깔고(로취 모드랑 위쳐센스 줌인해제 모드, 자동루팅 모드는 꼭 까세요 게임이 쾌적해짐) 영게롤트 모드까지 깔아 잘생긴 게롤트 얼굴 감상하면서 진행하니 어느새 메인스토리가 뚝딱 클리어됐다.

선택지에 따라 분기가 달라지는 스토리는 이미 다른 사람들이 손가락 쥐나도록 칭찬을 했으니 나도 더 첨언하지는 않겠지만 이것만이 위쳐3의 장점은 아니다.

2015년 게임이라고 생각되지 않을 정도로 아름다운 그래픽, 퀘스트 텍스트만으로도 모자라 지나가면서 말거는 시민 1까지도 해준 풀더빙, 개오지는 게롤트 얼굴과 목소리, 예쁘고 매력있는 여캐들, 선악이 없고 현실성 쩌는 끝도없는 서브퀘스트, RPG본연에 충실한 템파밍과 스킬트리 등등...

몇몇 모드를 곁들이면 그야말로 완벽한 게임이라고 말할 수 있다.

게다가 혜자스러운 DLC까지. 돈 뽑아먹을 생각밖에 안하는 한국 게임사들과 차원이 다르다.

나는 스팀 세일로 75%나 할인받아 DLC까지 합해서 16000원에 샀는데 솔직히 이 돈 주고 하기 미안할 지경이었다.

이렇게 혜자게임을 하다보니 지금까지 한국 게임에 쏟아부은 돈이 아까워져 다시는 3N사한테 내 돈을 쓰지 않으리라 다짐했다. 뻐킹 엿이나 처먹어ㅗㅗ

그리고 개인적으로 메인 스토리 깨면서 레벨대 맞는 서브스토리도 같이 깨는걸 추천한다.

나는 메인 빨리 밀고싶어서 메인을 위주로 하고 지금은 메인 다 밀고 서브만 주구장창 하고 있는데 맨날 기괴한 괴물들만 쳐잡고, 괴물 죽여줬더니 사람들이 통수나 치고, 지나가면 돌연변이라고 경멸하고, 벨렌하고 노비그라드에서는 허구한날 비와서 우중충하고, 파밍한다고 어두컴컴한 동굴들만 자꾸 가니 폐쇄공포증 올 것 같다.

ㄹㅇ로 위쳐 직업이 얼마나 우울증 오는 극한직업인지 절실히 느끼고있다.

메인퀘 할 때 만났던 트리스, 예니퍼, 시리, 베스미어, 램버트, 에스켈이 너무 보고싶다...

서브 다 밀고 빨리 젖과 꿀이 흐르는 투생으로 넘어가고싶다.


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DLC까지 모두 클리어 하고 난 후의 후기

무조건 고티에디션으로 사세요 ㅇㅇ DLC 혜자 갓겜 위쳐3!!!

하츠 오브 스톤, 블러드 앤 와인 둘 다 스토리 너무 탄탄하고 메인퀘, 서브퀘 분량 개오져서 각각 20시간 이상 뽑아먹기 쌉가능.

사람들은 보통 하츠 오브 스톤의 스토리를 더 쳐주던데 나는 개인적으로 블앤와가 더 재미있었다. 내 게롤트가 높으신 분한테 인정받고, 토너먼트 우승하면 우승자라고 대우해주면서 팬까지 생기고, 땅에 집까지 생기니 그렇게 뿌듯할수가 없다.

현실에서 하기 힘든 내집마련 게롤트라도 이루게 해줘야지.

뭣보다 블앤와의 메인 지역인 투생의 배경이 진짜진짜 너무너무 예쁘다. 클리어 하면서 스크린샷을 몇 장이나 찍었는지.

약스포이긴 한데 스토리 진행 중 갈 수 있는 동화 속 나라도 배경이 진짜 이뻐서... 특히 하늘의 그 무지개 너무이쁨. 스샷 엄청 많이 찍었다.

딱 한가지 단점은 성격 꼬인 피해자 코스프레 시아나보단 우리의 찬란한 전하 안나 헨리에타가 더 맘에 들었는데... 야스씬은 시아나밖에 없넹? 아쉽아쉽.

블앤와 못지 않게 하츠오브스톤도 재미있었다. 주역으로 나오는 올지어드가 했던 행동만 보면 개객끼인데 그래도 살아온 인생사를 들어보면 불쌍하기도 하고... 안쓰럽고...

위쳐에 나오는 등장인물들은 입체적인 인물들이 많아서 스토리를 다채롭게 만들어 주면서도 선택의 기로에 서는 플레이어들을 엄청나게 갈등하게 만든다.

얘를 죽여 살려, 도와 말아... 하지만 결국 돕게되는 마음따뜻한 위쳐 게롤트.

결국 만악의 근원은 군터 오딤이었으니 얘가 개객끼인걸로.

하츠오브스톤에서 메인 히로인으로 나오는 샤니도 맘에 들었다. 이쁘고 착하고 순진한 면이 있어서 귀엽다. 성격 개쎈 소서리스들 보다가 샤니를 보니 진짜 썸타는 느낌.

그리고 하츠오브스톤에서 제일 좋아하는 캐릭 블라디미르.

블라디미르 나오는 부분이 젤 재밌고 제일 웃김. 무게잡는 게롤트만 보다가 깨발랄한 블라디미르 보니 분위기 완전 반전되고 너무귀여움.

하여튼간에 위쳐3는 본편부터 DLC까지 하나도 버릴 것 없는 혜자 갓겜이니 아직 안해본 분이 계시다면 꼭 해보시길 바랍니다. 안한다면 게임인생에 어느정도 손해를 본다고 생각.

블앤와 마지막에 게롤트가 이제 쉬어야겠다고 말하는데 존나 아련해짐. 가지마 게롤트... 위쳐4 제작 기원합니다... 사이버펑크 살게 위쳐4 내줘...

Just finished the main story, took me 75 hours. I was not sure if I would like this game, since rpg games are not really my thing, but just after one hour I knew I will finish it. I played it on easy mode, so the gameplay itself was not the reason I enjoyed it so much, it was the story. Many characters, main and secondary quests, it just keeps going. I was playing it slow, taking my time with everything, exploring, doing side quests and treasure hunts, points of interest on the map. But about 50 hours in I just wanted to know what happens, how this story ends, so I just went for it but trust me, you can play this game for  100+ hours not even being close to the end of the story, it all depends on you. My map is still full of side quests, points of interest, witcher contracts, its insane. And then there are two dlcs as well.. Just one thing about the end of this story. Do some research on how to get a good one, but without spoilers. It it possible to find some information without ruining the game for you, looked it up after I finished it to see if I was lucky or not. I had the best ending possible, almost, but I was really close to getting a bad one, just one choice away. Of course, if you want to see where your choices will take the story, go for it.

Listen. I just played the whole trilogy from start to finish. I cannot recommend this game or any in the series to anyone. When a game has issues you fix them, you don't replace them with all new issues. CDPR spent too much time trying to find the mechanics they wanted to ever try improving on what they had and the result is that 1 has the best character customization in terms of number of choices, while 2 has the most meaningless choices, 3 tried to go back to 1's style but in a different way and ultimately came off as too simplified. The best controls go to 2, which was okay but still had some issues. All of them are walking simulator RPG's and should gain the tag Walking Simulator due to the lack of useful fast travel points built into the games themselves if any at all. If you don't enjoy walking/running or riding a horse that is barely faster than Geralt for five to ten minutes at a time don't buy this game or any in the series. What makes this game a hot mess is that the story even going from game to game required me to look to outside sources to connect the events. This is a big no-no that game developers need to stop doing. It didn't work well for Blizzard and it equally bad here. I shouldn't feel forced to purchase books or movies to follow a plot. That's just corporate greed attempting to milk it's customer base. This series in short made me angry and annoyed that a developer could make such terrible choices and get so much defense for it with people citing "characters" or "story" or "gameplay" all of which are subpar at best and irritating at worst. The real kick to the groin was when the games focused on people other than Geralt which was unfortunately unavoidable as the nature of the Witcher is that he is a sort of narrator or chronicler of events that the player sees the world through. Dandelion is annoying, Triss is annoying, Zoltan was a bore almost seemingly written to appease the "bro" crowd with his fratboy behavior, Shani being tossed into DLC was BS meant to milk fans, I'm not buying DLC even if the girl I chose in the first game is in it, not cool CDPR. Ciri was written like a common rebellious teen outside of her main story events and it was jarringly bad for someone with the wherewithal to plot a divide and conquer strategy to turn around and want to do bad things because the person we are doing them to is bad. It also flew in the face of many choices in the game that lead to an ending you can only get when you show her the value of being a decent human being, which according to the developers is a manic psychopath as one reaction is to vent like a ***** and throw crap around. Please note that I will never travel to Poland as apparently the way my oldest sister behaves is okay and I prefer to NOT have my car stolen to go kidnap children from child services. Finally let's talk about the overly simple plot every likes to tote around. It was common fantasy garbage and taking your sweet time in getting around to it doesn't make it any better. We have a chosen one which isn't you, done before, who has to save the world, done before, from an otherwise unstoppable force that no one can comprehend, done before. The journey was just as bad as the destination. Either the original works CDPR used aren't all that great or CDPR just stinks at making games. Either way I'm on to Cyberpunk this summer but I'm taking a break from that one and at least giving them a chance to fix their broken garbage.

I was saving this critique for last because I wanted this to end on a positive note. There are good things about this game. It's beautiful even now 6 years after release I find it has graphical quality that almost rivals modern AAA titles. Using graphics mods only makes it better and far tops even the most expensive AAA titles out there now. Well done graphics team. I also would like to take a moment to acknowledge how generally bug free a game of this size is. Compared to Skyrim or other massive world titles I expected so many bugs, I expected to fall through the ground at some point.

I would like to see CDPR make a homebrew IP instead of using other IP's at some point. It's clear they don't understand mechanics for the life of them but they can make some stunning visuals and managed to follow a janky slav story long enough to hammer out three games from it's novels. Especially if the titles are small enough to let them make an in house IP of it without stretching the budget.

Personally my #1 game of all time. It looks beautiful, it has a great story (read the books and play the two games before this to have the ultimate experience), combat is nice with the signs. I especially love how it feels like (and more often than not) every decision you make impacts some part of the world in a good or bad way. It is also a very harsh world, forcing you to choose between two evils. This coupled with the fact that your decisions actually impact the world makes is so that you always have to be on your toes. The world also has tiny details which gives it life. If there is one gripe I had it is that I couldn't make it harder, completing my second playthrough on the hardest difficulty (and every subsequent one) but it's not meant to be a Souls game (and if you want that level of difficulty go play those games) so I can't fault it for that.

Would highly recommend picking up mods like AutoLoot and Friendly HUD to make exploring easier and less of a hassle, but they aren't vital to core game play. Wiedźmin 3: Dziki gon

Legendarna gra bazowana na książkach autora Andrzeja Sapkowskiego która wzbudziła na wielu twarzach ludzi wielki zachwyt.

Gra ukłania się nam wynagradzając nam elementy takie jak:
Świetna towarzysząca nam muzyka przez całą podróż po świecie, walkach.
Bardzo dobra grafika która ma bardzo zadbane detale.
Otwarty świat, Wiele misji zleceń, Oraz ogrom eksploracji.
Fantastyczna fabuła która wzniosła płomień wielu graczy.
Bardzo dobry system walk zważając na opiniach ludzi, który jest zbalansowany oraz współpracuję z elementami alchemii, Oraz kontekstu RPG

Świat Wiedźmina to jest fantastyczna kraina która zaniesie ciebie w szeroką podróż, Ścieżka jest dowolna, Panuje tutaj duża swoboda oraz wolność wyboru.

Wiedźmin 3 Dziki gon jest naprawdę grą wartą zagrania. W promocji, czy bez.
Gra nie jest perfekcyjna. Występują tutaj również błędy czy glicze. Na przykład płotka, Druh wiedźmina :)

Ale podczas rozgrywki, Błędów nie ma dużo a nawet prawie ich nie widać. Więc jaki jest twój wybór?
Dołącz do armii i staw czoło Dzikiemu gonowi.

Tej wojny nikt za nas nie wygra!
Bliski jest czas szaleństwa i czas pogardy!

Эту игру хвалили уже столько, что я не смогу подобрать слов для описания всего хорошего в ней. Так что просто скажу: хорошая игра. Правда.

에픽에서 어크시리즈 사다가 해보면서 특히 오디세이는 선택지에 따라서 추후 달라지는 점들이 좋았었다.
근데 문제는 긍정과 부정의 차이가 명확했고 긍정은 긍정의결과를 낳고 부정은 부정의 결과를 낳는다.

그러나 위쳐는 나의 선택이 의도에 따라서 결정되지 않는 경우가 빈번했고
그건 나에게 실망감과 만족감 그리고 되돌아보게되는 그러한 것들을 불러 일으켰다.

그런점에서 어쌔신크리드는 2회차로 충분히 만족할만한 게임이었지만
위쳐3는 적어도 3회차 까지는 즐겁게 해볼수 있지 않나 싶다.

물론 단점이나 아쉬운점도 분명 존재했으나 살아서 움직이는 소설책 한권을 읽는 내게는 아무런 방해도 되지 않았다.

세일때 사라는 사람들도 많은데 물론 세일때 사면 좋긴하다.
그러나 구매할거면 GOTY를 반드시 구매하고 (쓸대없는 사운드트랙을 제외한 모든게포함됨)
정가를 주고 구매해도 가격대비 품질이 너무나도 뛰어나기 때문에 거저 얻은 기분을 느낄수 있다.

최소한의 찍먹족 이라면 족히 50시간은 즐길수 있고
완벽히 정복하는 사람들은 100시간 이상도 즐길수있다.

哥哥刚想说些什么，却被一只玉手捂住了嘴。“嘘...”妹妹把头凑到哥哥耳边，轻声说：“.人家想和你一起..起...建设中国特色社会主义嘛。”

只感到一股社会责任感与民族归属感冲上了他的头，于是双手开始不自觉的对妹妹薄薄的睡衣实行伟大的改革开放政策，牢牢把握一个中心两个基本点，刺激消费扩大内需，让一切创造财富的源泉涌流。

“啊.....那里不行...我的社会主义核心价值观..要缺失...”

话音未落，哥哥那高速增长的 GDP 已经在经济特区开始了社会主义现代化建设，深入贯彻落实“三个代表”重要思想，落实科学发展观，以人为本推动经济社会发展，围绕主题抓住主线，全面提高开放型经济水平，坚持“引进来”和“走出去”相结合的战略，优化结构，拓展深度，极大地促进了社会生产力的发展。

而妹妹则结合具体国情新出台了紧缩性财政政策缓解通货膨胀，吸纳了大量社会流动资金，取得了卓越成效。“越来越紧了呢，又促进经济稳定持续增长了呢...”收获了生活水平不断提高的哥哥的一致好评。

本着为人民服务的工作态度，妹妹依旧沉浸在爱岗敬业的无限快乐中。“以后...一定要...对人民负.责...哦，不然..我可要..建立信息公开制度..与办事公开...制度了哟。”

“明明那么享受中国梦... 舆论还不老实,看我不加快转变经济发展方式好好思想教育一下这不听话的妹妹。”于是哥哥直起身来实施创新驱动发展战略，增强创新发展新动力，深化改革，加快转变经济发展方式的主攻方向，促进资本在全球范围内的流动；牢牢扭住经济建设为中心，聚精会神搞建设，一心一意谋发展，推动了城乡发展一体化。

妹妹日益增长的物质文化需求终于无法忍受：“啊...要全面建成...小康社会了...实现...共产义...了...好幸福...”

“那我也要实现伟大中国梦了。”哥哥通过市场准入规则后，实行扩张型经济政策，使大量流动资金流入市场，缓解通货紧缩，资金全部吸入市场，达到泡沫顶峰。

“...全部投入市场了呢，这下糟糕了...”妹妹一脸幸福地说。“还没完呢!“哥哥再次蓬勃发展的经济水平又一次的抬起了头。。“啊？！啊...”

天已经亮了，但哥哥却依然在贯彻从群众中来到群众中去的工作方法...

Such a banger game. I can't express to anybody enough how good this game was. The crafting and brewing system may seem intimidating at first for a complete noob, but to be honest, I only had to use crafting and brewing for like one mission.  I was the person who tried the game out for a couple hours, then thought it wasn't going anywhere so I refunded it. That was a huge mistake. One day I thought about it and was like "You know what, I really want to get into this game", so I got it again, powered through it and am so glad that I did. It is kind of rough getting started, like grinding to the recommended level for the first contract you do, but after that, your levels just start taking off. So that's some advice. All in all, 10/10 game, I also couldn't be more happy with the ending.

이런, 게임을 해보면 알겠지만 이 게임은 정말 명작입니다. 반지의 제왕이 그렇듯 장대한 세계관을 구축하고 거기서 매력있는 캐릭터와 중세의 분위기는 좋습니다. 물론 위쳐3의 경우 원작이 있다지만 이런 장대한 이야기에 플레이어를 집어넣는 이런 디테일은 정말 미쳤고 그것만으로도 명작이라는 말을 듣기에 부족함이 없습니다. 높은 디테일에 방대한 분량은 정말 좋습니다. 

하지만 너무 기대를 해서일까요. 이 게임이 저에게 지루한 경험인것이 아쉽습니다. 스토리는 완성도 있습니다. 하지만 길이가 너무 방대하고 텀이 긴 편이라 몰입하기 힘듭니다. 그거 아시나요? 세기의 명작 반지의 제왕의 첫 한두권은 나즈굴을 피해 숲을 지나가는 간단한 사건에 숲에 대한 온갖 묘사가 곁들어서 1~2권분량으로 제작되었다는것을요. 

 액션 게임을 표방하고 있다면 스토리 컷신의 피로감을 적절한 액션으로 순환시켜야 합니다. 이 게임의 전투는 제가 해봤던 모든(턴제게임을 포함해서!!!) 게임중 가장 지루합니다. 모션도, 손맛도, 원거리싸움도, 스킬의 전략적인 활용도 거의 없다시피하고 평타,회피가 끝입니다. 그저 스펙이 높으면 이기고 스펙이 낮으면 지는 단순한 액션입니다.

 또한 저는 오픈월드의 경우 또다른 발견과 이동의 자유라는점에서 가산점이 들어간다고 생각합니다. 하지만 위쳐3는 발견에 대한 기대감이 적습니다. 발견의 경우 두가지 상황이 생기는데 첫째는 이게임 최대 단점이라고 생각하는 전투, 둘째는 사이드퀘스트입니다. 사이드퀘스트의 경우 잘 음미하면 그안에 들어있는 내용물은 물론 다른 게임과 비교해서 최고의 스토리를 보여주지만, 게임에 질려버렸다면 지루한 컷신과 지루한 전투의 반복입니다. 악순환으로 빠지는 거지요. 중요하지 않는 대사를 무의식적으로 스킵하다가 막상 중요한 부분까지 넘기게 됩니다.

이동의 자유도 없습니다. 갈수 있는곳은 레벨 제한으로 정해져있고 시리를 찾다보면 결국 제작자가 원하는 단 한가지 방향으로 이동하게 됩니다. 선택이 아닌 순서만이 달라진채로 말이죠. 대화에서 선택의 자유를 줘놓고 이동의 자유를 뺏으니 상대적으로 밋밋합니다.

이 게임은 완성도가 높고 방대한 세계관으로 플레이어를 끌어들이는 명작이 맞습니다. 하지만 취향에 안맞아 한번 몰입이 깨지면 또 한번 위쳐의 세계에 초대받기 힘듭니다.

[h1]有史以来最好的RPG游戏[/h1]
一周目对于支线探索的不多，主要就是一路冲主线，昆特牌也没打，不到50小时就打完了游戏本体，当时只是被游戏的剧情和演出所震撼。
放假之后重新捡起了巫师三，开始玩DLC和二周目，静下心来深入体验之后才发现这款游戏对于庞大世界观的构建有多么的高明。不论是支线、委托，还是寻宝任务，都是在对于世界观的完善。在提升难度后，每次猎魔前的搜集情报、准备药水和剑油、观察攻击方式并且进行对应的调整，游戏变得更像[b]“猎魔人”[/b]而不是一味的砍杀和闪避。当你静下心来，去一本本浏览收集到的书籍、信件等等文字时，又是对游戏中已经构建好的世界观的不断补充。
这才是RPG该有的游戏氛围，让你沉浸到游戏搭建的世界观当中，去体会、体验，而不是单纯的“看”。只能说玩到巫师三这样的游戏是一种幸运。

Average, somewhat lackluster gameplay bolstered by a great story with characters you (mostly) care about.  TBH, while I liked the game overall (evidenced by my 140 hour play time), the controls and underwhelming action are what kept me from being hyped about Cyberpunk 2077.  I figured if they couldn't get 3rd person swordplay right after three games, they would probably have some issues trying to switch to a first person shooter having no experience making them.  Not to say I won't ever get CP2077, but it will probably be in a year or two after a lot of patches are available....just like I waited for this game.  
BTW...Triss is way hotter than Yennefer, and infinitely more likable.  
Also, the scariest looking monsters in the game are the children.  DESTROY THEM ALL WITH IGNI!!!!

[h1]ㄹㅇ 최고의 게임이다[/h1]

[b]스토리[/b]
%위쳐 1,2를 한적이 없기에 초반엔 그저 게임 플레이에 집중하였습니다.%

[list]
[*]스토리는 매우 좋았습니다. 초반부터 후반까지 있는 위쳐만의 뽕 자체가 매우 좋았습니다(사실 이거떄문에 위쳐 끝까지함 ㅋㅋㄹㅃㅃ)

[*]메인퀘스트와 서브퀘스트 둘다 각각의 스토리가 매력이 있었습니다. 그리고 dlc들의 스토리는 진짜...............캬 취한다
단, 메인스토리 마지막 부분의 [spoiler]시리를 살리기 위한[/spoiler] 중요 선택지들은 한국의 정서와는 맞지 않았습니다.(옳지 않다는것이 아니라, 문화의 차이를 말하는 겁니다)
[/list]

[b]게임플레이[/b]
%게임 패드를 사용한 기준입니다. 키마는 차이가 있을수 있습니다%

[list]
[*]조작감의 경우는 영 좋지 않지만 하기에 그지같지는 않았습니다.(타고 다니는 말의 경우도) "나는 이런 조작감으로는 못하겠다!" 하시는 분들은 조작감을 살짝 개선해주는 모드를 설치하시는것을 추천합니다.

[*]타격감의 경우엔 아쉽습니다.  저는 개인적으로 타격감에 그렇게 신경을 쓰진 않았지만 제 친구의 경우는 타격감 떄문에 위쳐3를 그렇게 좋아하진 않았습니다.(물론 춤추는듯한 검술이라고는 하고 개인적으로는 싫어하지 않는 모션이지만 타격감의 경우엔 실드치기가 좀.......) 타격감의 경우는 모드를 깔더라도 개인적으로는 차이를 느끼지 못해 타격감에 민감하신 분이시라면 감수하시거나 플레이를 비추드립니다. 

[*]퀘스트의 표시 방식이 맘에 들지 않았습니다. 지도상에 표시가 되지 않고 반드시 퀘스트를 선택해야 경로가 나와 내 위치에서 가까이 있는 미션을 찾기가 어려웠습니다. 이 경우엔 퀘스트 위치를 지도에 표시해주는 모드 설치를 강력 권장드립니다.
[/list]

Released in a time when companies were dead set on carbon copied quest fillers, the most enjoyable part of the Witcher for me is the refreshing fact that every quest, small or big, is hand crafted and unique. They even model out totally unique races and monsters for single side quests (such as the Sylvan or Godling). The attention and care given in this regard amplifies all of the other incredible qualities this game has.

Story and plot are exciting, relationships are unique, branching decisions have significant impact on outcomes, and combat has diverse mechanics for sword fighting, alchemy and magic that are best when combined. Some examples are:

You can pursue a romantic relationship with a character, realize they have used you for alternate motives and as a player decide to interpret this as benign politics or personal betrayal. Then the consequences of your words could restore that person to your side or end with a fight to the death. Either may be justifiable to your viewpoint and will affect long term game play.

Additionally when you approach combat you could go in blindly swinging your sword and likely deal with a difficult encounter, or you can read your bestiary around the monster (discovered in perhaps a lost tome) and apply proper oil to your blade, use appropriate defensive magics and decoctions to make the fight fair. When you realize this is the way of the Witcher, study and pre-fight tactics, you begin to grow more powerful than simply dumping points into attack power.

Some areas of the game that are a bit weaker are perhaps the loot and crafting aspects. There is generally not enough income in the game to craft or brew everything. Witcher contracts generally provide lower pay than you can really use to upgrade long term and will fall back to the age-old RPG tradition of hoarding loot up to your inventory limit and selling at various traders (which also just don't have that much currency). It was in this area I recommend getting the MOD: Double Quest Rewards which I feel is more balanced. Additionally it may seem counter intuitive, but paying blacksmiths to break down looted equipment will save you money in crafting more powerful items in the long term. Combine this with foot travel to collect the various herbs in the world to save a lot on Alchemy costs. Some of this balancing is not really learned until your second playthrough.

I have played all three Witcher series games, each nothing less than excellent. Witcher 1 was more linear and really shined through its story and dark world. Witcher 2 best compared to Dragon Age, somewhat "open world" areas with side missions but of course that powerful storyline. Witcher 3 however has re-scoped the expectations of the genre that I can safely say has not been outdone to this day (2021). Even CD Project Red's latest Cyberpunk game (when focusing on story, world building, hand-crafted quests and not on the launch issues) does not reach the levels achieved by Witcher 3 the Wild Hunt.

This game is a masterpiece, rife with quite possibly the best story I've encountered in gaming. Humor. Romance. Nostalgia. What does this game not have? Heck, Witcher 3 even has a fully-fleshed out card game within the game that in itself is exceptional. Whether you play for completion or the story, this game will excite you. At the hardest difficulty, the game can feel extremely daunting, especially at the beginning as you begin to understand enemy patterns and the intricate combat mechanics. Not to mention, this game is visually breathtaking.

There's not many games that live up to expectations, especially those as lofty as the Witcher 3 has received, but even 5-6 years later, this game met and truthfully surpassed those expectations. 

Even the smallest of side characters feel well developed and significant. And Geralt. Oh my. Despite his often monotonous and gravelly tone, there is just so much character and wit in every action. There's also no better way to say this than that the story makes sense. Nothing feels like filler. And the actions you take feel like they matter and are appropriate actions to take given the circumstances. The DLC is also fantastic.

CDPR and every other gaming company should strive to achieve what the Witcher 3 has.

I'm a die-hard fan of the Fallout series. I've never played anything w/ Swords and Sorcery since my D&D days back in the 1980s. I LOVE The  Witcher 3!
Thanks to a massively powerful PC, I'm able to play this at Ultra settings across the board. The visuals are stunning, both in gameplay and during cut-scenes. The combat is fun and fluid, the monsters are scary as heck the first time you run into one of their kind, and the mechanics are simple. It took me a few levels to get the hang of placing character points and properly dealing w/ traders, herbalists, etc., but now it's a piece of cake.
The writing - both in spoken dialogue and the books/pages you find - are first-rate. I judge these by a simple rule: does it seem natural - like if that's what that character would say/write - or does it come across as someone sitting at a keyboard and typing the first thing that comes to their head? In both cases in The Witcher 3 I can say, without hesitation, that it's the former by a country mile.
The conversation trees are incredibly fun. You can decide to play coy, mean, or just be direct. I can't wait to play several times through again in order to check out the avenues the speech choices I didn't make this time i order to see where they lead me.
I have the expansion packs but haven't tried them out yet. I'm definitely looking forward to them.
Get this game! You won't be sorry.

내가 게임 살 때는 본편만 세일해서 본편만 샀었는데 나중에는 dlc까지 합본을 그 가격에 팔더라... 배아파서 원.. 처음에는 도대체가 이게 왜 그렇게까지 띵작이라고 칭송 받는지 몰랐는데 돈 아까워서 그냥 하다보니까 어느샌가 몰입하고 있는 내가 있음. 할 컨텐츠가 너무나도 많아서 게임 켜는 게 무서웠음ㅋㅋㅋ 한 번 켜서하면 3,4시간은 순삭이었으니까... 처음에는 노트북 혹사시키면서 30시간 플레이하다가 데스크탑 맞춰서 다시 처음부터 했는데 정확시 총 플레이 시간 100시간만에 본편 엔딩을 봤음. 처음 30시간 제외하면 70시간만에 본편 엔딩 본 거기도 한데 천천히 주변에 생기는 서브 퀘스트도 하나하나 대사 스킵 안하고 읽다보니 꽤 걸리기도 한듯. 본편을 워낙 재미있게 깨서 나중에 익스팬션 패스까지 사서 했는데 이제서야 엔딩을 봄. 스팀 시간으로 총 122시간에다가 스위치로도 사서 했는데 스위치로 10시간 이상 했다고 적힌 거 보니 대충 dlc만 30시간 안팎으로 한듯. 너무나도 재미있게 깼고 스위치로도 심심하면 간간히 들어와서 회차 돌 듯. 전투는 재미없다는 평이 많은데 사실 맞음. 무슨 생각으로 무적기를 준 건지 모르겠네. 그치만 노래랑 분위기가 전투가 재미 없는 걸 커버할정도로 좋기 때문에 딱히 크게 신경 쓰이지는 않았음. 물론 초반에는 대체 이게 뭐야 싶을 정도로 노잼이었지만... 아무튼 정말로 다들 그래픽카드와 cpu가 버틸 수만 있다면 한 번씩은 꼭 해봤으면 좋겠는 작품. 이렇게 잘만들었으면서 사이버펑크는 왜 그렇게 낸거야..?!?! 엔딩 보고 뽕 차올라서 횡설수설 적었는데 읽느라 고생들 하셨고 곧 새해인데 여기까지 읽으신 분들 새해복 많이들 받으시길.

One of, if not the best game I've played. I got immersed, i cried, i got mad, i laughed, and i enjoyed almost every moment of this game. It may be a long game, but its worth it. The side quests can be fun, annoying and tedius, but often give more to the world, story, or arsenal. Speaking of Arsenals, the amount of weapons, items, potions and bombs make combat never the same. Sure you have a silver sword for monsters, but what if you cast a spell then threw some bombs at that monster, or what about shooting it from atop your horse with a crossbow while avoiding its deadly strikes. You can prepare for every monster and situation, from monsters to humans, you wont lack in tools to kill.

While cdpr's latest game may be shit, this one is gold. Well worth the money spent, and time spent.

Not a fan of RPGs mostly. I actually put this game on the ignore list for a long time so I didn't have steam recommend it every time I visited the store page. I bought cyberpunk thinking I'll try that because I like FPSs and Fallout 3 was a lot of fun to me back in the day. I uninstalled it after the first few hours because of bugs and just generally wanting the game to be better. Then I revisited this store page. It was the winter sale. 15 bucks for an entire game and DLCs with people saying just get it? Okay

I am now 40 hours in after 5 days and I actually don't know where the time is going. This is one of the greatest games I have ever played and I am barely half way through it. I never knew an RPG can make you feel like you're actually in a story. It's incredible. And the intuitive fight mechanics make you want to get better and better at fighting. I feel like a god when I can pull off combos against multiple enemies without being touched. 

10/10

I bought the Game of the Year edition and it was one of the best bought game, hell, it is THE Best game that i have ever bought. I heard of the witcher game population, and it is so much more after i played it.

The story was splendidly written, the main quest, side quest and all, never a point where you got lost or bored of the main quest and decide to do other things u could in game, though there are many side quest and discovery in this game but ALL of them are decent. The DLC quest by far is the one that i felt its not a cash grabber: the depth and the plot , characters design and all are decent designed that you wish for even more....

Gameplay is kind of a action RPG, with decent combat system and level up to unlock new skill etc... 

As for soundtrack, " otherworldy amazing " isn't enough to even describe the slightest of it

With the release of Cyberpunk 2077 i notice there were complains regarding to Witcher 3's plot being too long, and yet for some fans, including myself i would say otherwise.

Gameplay      9.5/10
Soundtrack   9.7/10
Graphic           9/10
Story          100/10

Conclusion: It's a must for a gamer, It's an adventure one shalt never miss

I want to start by saying that I have played a lot of games so far, I am more of a shooter-player. But I also like RPGs, a few years ago I first tried to play Witcher 3, I didn't understand anything and it seemed very boring, I deleted it and reinstalled it to give it a try, but nothing. Until at one point I played somewhere at 20 hours and I didn't leave the game at all, I stayed for 5-6 hours each day and I didn't realize how fast time passes. I was one with the game, I felt every emotion and state that the game sent, I don't even know what to say about the soundtrack. It's just brilliant, the story of Wildhunt gave me goosebumps, and not to mention DLC. Simply exceptional (Blood and Wine) being the best.
10/10 - a great game that will remain with me a long time.

It's surprisingly large with a lot of open world opportunity, but also a clear path for linear progression. The characters and lore are fascinating, engaging and keep you wanting more. The game play is easy enough to understand to progress, but has layers for additional difficulty and is very rewarding to stray off course and battle/collect things. There's a TON to do, but you never feel inundated by the vastness, it's a very happy medium with a lot to love.

Like most open-world games the only criticism I'd have is the world feels 'empty' and the quests feel repetitive after a while. I found myself skipping cut-scenes more and more about halfway through. Probably the greatest RPG I have ever played. I cannot think of a single grievance with the game aside from a few bugs here and there that force you to load up a recent save. After 4-5 years the game still holds up really well graphically and mechanically. Sure the combat can be a bit tedious and/or one dimensional, but there are plenty of choices to change the combat/encounters to suit your liking. The Witcher lore essentially adapts Scandinavian folk lore, myths, and legends into a living universe, so there are a lot of familiar creatures an story beats, but with bit of a twist. Basically modern Brothers Grimm.

Highly recommend 20 out of 10

I have hundreds of hours on TW3WH on separate consoles. I've played through all the expansions and base game twice over and I'm on my third playthrough now. There should be no doubt this game is worth playing. It's almost notorious now for being TOO well received to the point it's being called "overhyped". Do I think It's overhyped? Not at all. Without a doubt this game is the closest thing to a masterpiece I've ever played. the negative reviews I've seen have come from people who couldn't "get into it". In my opinion if you haven't finished a games story you shouldn't review it. Although I very well know how difficult it can be for people with busy schedules. this game is LONG. 80-100 hours min. to see the credits roll. maybe less if you're only doing story missions and blowing through them, but for a first playthrough you better believe you're going to do side missions because sometimes they are far more interesting than some points of the story.
Let me put it this way, this game that I've played twice I bought again for $15 with all the dlc's, and honestly I feel guilty that i couldn't pay $60 for the game and $20 for each expansion like I had originally.
